continual.definition: adj. 持续不断的；频繁的（可能有间断）
continual.memory_trick: continu(e)（继续）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 一再继续的 → 持续不断的
continual.example: His continual practice is key to his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

continuous.memory_trick: continu(e)（继续）+ ous（形容词后缀）→ 一直继续的 → 连续不断的
continuous.example: His continuous exposure to different cultures enhances his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

continue.memory_trick: con（共同）+ tinu（保持）→ 共同保持下去 → 继续
continue.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he plans to continue studying more.

conform.memory_trick: 联想 'con-'（共同）+ 'form'（形式）→ 使形式共同一致 → 符合规范
conform.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him conform to different cultural environments.

inform.memory_trick: 联想 'in-'（进入）+ 'form'（形式）→ 使知识进入形态 → 告知
inform.example: Research informs us that immersive practice is key to learning new languages quickly.

information.memory_trick: 联想 'in-'（内部）+ 'form'（形式）+ '-ation'（名词后缀）→ 赋予内部形态的内容 → 信息
information.example: He gathered information on language learning techniques to improve his ability to learn new languages quickly.

reform.definition: v. & n. 改革，改良，改造
reform.memory_trick: re-（再）+ form（形成）→ 再次形成，重塑 → 改革。
reform.example: The government introduced a series of policies to reform the education system.

confront.definition: vt. 面对，遭遇；对抗，对峙
confront.memory_trick: con-(共同) + front(前面) → 共同站到前面 → 面对面 → 面对，对抗。
confront.example: We must confront the outdated regulations and work to change them.

infer.memory_trick: in-（向内）+ fer（带来）→ 从已知信息中“把结论带出来” → 推断。
infer.example: From his tone, I can infer that he is not satisfied with the result.

inference.definition: n. 推断，推论；推断的结果
inference.memory_trick: infer（推断）的名词形式。记住：-ence 是常见的名词后缀。
inference.example: His inference about the cause of the problem was based on careful observation.

preferable.memory_trick: pre-（在前，优先）+ fer（带来）+ -able（可…的）→ 可以被优先带来的 → 更可取的。
preferable.example: A quiet environment is preferable when you need to concentrate.

preference.definition: n. 偏爱，偏好；优先选择
preference.memory_trick: prefer（更喜欢）的名词形式。pre-（优先）+ fer（带来）+ -ence（名词后缀）→ 优先带来的东西 → 偏爱。
preference.example: He has a strong preference for classical music over pop music.

refer.definition: v. 提到，谈及；参考，查阅；提交，转交
refer.memory_trick: re-（回，再）+ fer（带来）→ 把话题或问题“带回到”某处 → 提及、参考或提交给某人处理。
refer.example: Please refer to the user manual if you encounter any technical issues.

evolve.definition: v. 进化；演变；逐步发展
evolve.memory_trick: e-（向外）+ volv（滚动）→ 向外滚动 → 逐步展开、演变。联想：生物从简单形态“滚动”发展成复杂形态。
evolve.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a skill that may evolve from consistent practice and exposure.

evolution.definition: n. 进化；演变；发展过程
evolution.memory_trick: evolve 的名词形式，-tion 表名词。联想：evolution 是 evolve 这个“滚动过程”的结果或状态。
evolution.example: The evolution of language learning methods has made it easier for people to acquire new skills quickly.

revolve.definition: v. 旋转；围绕；以…为中心
revolve.memory_trick: re-（回、再）+ volv（滚动）→ 来回滚动 → 旋转、循环。联想：地球绕着太阳“滚动”（公转）。
revolve.example: His language learning strategy seems to revolve around daily conversation practice and vocabulary accumulation.

relate.definition: v. 讲述，叙述；使有联系，将…联系起来；涉及，有关
relate.memory_trick: re-（回）+ late（带来，来自拉丁语'latus'）→ 把故事带回来 → 讲述；把事物带回到一起 → 联系。
relate.example: Can you relate the main events of the story in your own words?

relationship.definition: n. （人、团体、国家之间的）关系，关联；恋爱关系
relationship.memory_trick: relation（关系）+ ship（表示状态、性质的名词后缀）→ 关系的状态，尤指人际间的具体关联。
relationship.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which greatly enhances his relationship with people from different cultures.

relative.definition: adj. 相对的，比较的；有关的 n. 亲戚，亲属
relative.memory_trick: relat(e)（联系）+ ive（形容词/名词后缀）→ 有联系的 → 相对的；有血缘联系的人 → 亲戚。
relative.example: The concept of 'difficulty' is relative; what is hard for one person might be easy for another.

relativity.memory_trick: relativ(e)（相对的）+ ity（名词后缀，表性质）→ 相对的性质。特指爱因斯坦的“相对论”。
relativity.example: Einstein's theory of relativity revolutionized our understanding of space, time, and gravity.

relation.definition: n. 关系，联系；亲属，亲戚
relation.memory_trick: relate（联系）的名词形式。指事物或人之间的“关联”被带（late）到一起（re-）。
relation.example: There is a close relation between regular exercise and improved mental health.

conscious.definition: adj. 有意识的，神志清醒的；意识到的，察觉的
conscious.memory_trick: con-(共同，加强) + sci(知道) + -ous(形容词后缀) → 完全知道的 → 有意识的。
conscious.example: He is conscious of his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

mode.memory_trick: 直接源自词根 'mod'（方式）。联想：做事的特定度量（mod）或规范出来的方式。
mode.example: The camera has an automatic mode that adjusts the settings for optimal photos.

model.definition: n. 模型；模范；模特 v. 模仿；塑造
model.memory_trick: 拆解：mod（尺度，样式）+ -el（名词后缀，表示'小'或'工具'）。联想：一个按特定尺度（mod）制作的小样或典范。
model.example: She used clay to model a miniature version of the ancient building.

moderate.definition: adj. 适度的；中等的；温和的 v. 使缓和；主持
moderate.memory_trick: 拆解：moder（=mod，尺度）+ -ate（形容词/动词后缀）。联想：使保持在中间尺度（mod）的，不极端。
moderate.example: The government called for a moderate increase in spending to avoid inflation.

modest.definition: adj. 谦虚的；适度的；不大的
modest.memory_trick: 拆解：mod（尺度）+ -est（形容词后缀）。联想：言行保持在合适尺度（mod）内的，不过分。
modest.example: Despite his great success, he remained modest about his achievements.

modify.memory_trick: 拆解：mod（尺度，方式）+ -ify（动词后缀，表示'使...'）。联想：使符合新的尺度（mod）或方式。
modify.example: The engineer had to modify the design to meet the new safety standards.

modern.definition: adj. 现代的；新式的；时髦的
modern.memory_trick: 拆解：mod（方式，尺度）+ -ern（形容词后缀）。联想：符合当前时代标准（mod）的方式或样式。
modern.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a skill highly valued in the modern globalized world.

modernization.definition: n. 现代化；现代化进程
modernization.memory_trick: 拆解：modern（现代的）+ -ization（名词后缀，表示'...化'）。联想：使事物达到现代（modern）标准（mod）的过程。
modernization.example: The rapid modernization of the country's infrastructure has boosted economic growth.

approach.definition: 1. 动词：接近、靠近；着手处理、探讨。2. 名词：方法、途径；接近的过程。
approach.memory_trick: 拆解为 ap-（加强）+ proach（接近），强调“主动靠近或接触某事物”，可联想为“一种接近问题的方式”。
approach.example: He has developed a systematic approach to learn new languages quickly.

approval.definition: n. 批准，同意；认可，赞成
approval.memory_trick: 联想：approve（批准）+ al（名词后缀）→ 批准的行为或状态。核心是“心理上接近或接受某种观点或请求”。
approval.example: His innovative method for language learning received official approval from the academic committee due to its effectiveness.

approve.definition: v. 批准，通过；赞成，同意
approve.memory_trick: 拆分：ap（加强）+ prove（测试，证明）→ 经过测试证明后予以接受 → 批准。词源上与“接近”相关，表示使想法或提案被接受（接近共识）。
approve.example: The board decided to approve his proposed approach to accelerated language learning after seeing the remarkable results.

influence.memory_trick: 拆解：in-（向内）+ flu（flow，流）+ -ence（名词后缀）→ 古代观念认为星象之气流入人体而产生影响 → 引申为“影响力”。可与inflate联想：都是“向内注入某种东西（气或力）”。
influence.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a talent that may influence his career opportunities globally.

influential.definition: adj. 有影响的；有权势的
influential.memory_trick: influence（影响） + -ial（形容词后缀）→ 有影响的。注意重音移位。
influential.example: She is an influential scholar in the field of cognitive linguistics.

predict.definition: vt. 预言；预测；预示；预知
predict.memory_trick: 拆解为 pre- (在...之前) + dict (说) → 在事情发生之前就说出来 → 预测。可以联想：天气预报员在天气变化 '之前' 就 '说' 出来。
predict.example: Based on his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, we can predict he will master Spanish within a few months.

displace.definition: vt. 取代，替代；迫使…离开家园，使背井离乡
displace.memory_trick: dis-（离开）+ place（位置）→ 使离开原位 → 取代；使流离失所
displace.example: The new technology quickly displaced the older methods in the industry.

disorder.definition: n. 混乱，杂乱；失调，疾病 vt. 扰乱，使失调
disorder.memory_trick: dis-（否定）+ order（秩序）→ 没有秩序 → 混乱
disorder.example: The sudden noise caused a disorder in the classroom, disrupting the lesson.

preparation.definition: n. 准备，预备；准备工作；制剂
preparation.memory_trick: pre-（前）+ par（准备）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 事先做的准备。
preparation.example: Careful preparation is key to his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

preliminary.definition: adj. 初步的，预备的 n. 初步行动；预赛
preliminary.memory_trick: pre-（前）+ limin（门槛，起始）+ ary（...的）→ 在门槛之前的 → 预备的。
preliminary.example: The team conducted preliminary tests before starting the main experiment.

premier.definition: adj. 首要的，第一的 n. 总理，首相
premier.memory_trick: prem（第一，首要）+ ier（表人或物）→ 处于第一位置的人或事物。
premier.example: The premier league is the top division of the football competition.

confer.definition: v. 授予（学位、荣誉等）；商讨，商议
confer.memory_trick: con-（共同）+ fer（带来）→ 把各自的意见“带来”到一起 → 共同商讨；也可理解为“把荣誉带给某人” → 授予。
confer.example: The committee will confer to decide on the best candidate for the award.

predecessor.definition: n. 前任；前辈；原有事物
predecessor.memory_trick: pre-（前）+ decess（走，离开）+ or（人）→ 先前离开的人 → 前任。
predecessor.example: The new manager quickly learned from the mistakes of his predecessor.

preface.definition: n. 前言，序言 vt. 以...为开端；为...加序言
preface.memory_trick: pre-（前）+ face（面，表面）→ 写在正文前面的话 → 前言。
preface.example: The author wrote a thoughtful preface to explain the book's origins.

prejudice.definition: n. 偏见，成见 vt. 使有偏见；损害
prejudice.memory_trick: pre-（预先）+ judice（判断）→ 事先做出的判断 → 偏见。
prejudice.example: The judge warned the jury to avoid any prejudice against the defendant.

premise.definition: n. 前提；房屋及地基 vt. 以...为前提
premise.memory_trick: pre-（前）+ mise（送出，放置）→ 预先放置好的东西 →（逻辑的）前提；（法律）上述房屋（文件前面列出的）。
premise.example: The entire argument is based on a false premise.

premium.definition: n. 保险费；额外费用；溢价 adj. 优质的；高价的
premium.memory_trick: pre-（前）+ em（拿，取）→ 需要事先支付或获取的东西 → 保险费；额外费用。
premium.example: This brand charges a premium for its high-quality products.

confusion.definition: n. 困惑，混淆；混乱，骚乱
confusion.memory_trick: 拆解：con-（共同）+ fus（倾倒、混合）+ -ion（名词后缀）→ 把很多东西“倒在一起混合”→ 导致“混乱、困惑”。联想：把不同颜色的液体倒进一个杯子，混成一团，分不清彼此，就是“混淆/困惑”的状态。
confusion.example: His remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly caused some confusion among his classmates, who struggled to keep up.

confuse.definition: v. 使困惑，使糊涂；混淆，弄混
confuse.memory_trick: 拆解：con-（共同）+ fuse（倾倒、混合）→ 把东西“混在一起”→ 使人“分不清、搞糊涂”。联想：把盐和糖混在一起（pour together），别人就“confuse”了哪个是哪个。
confuse.example: He did not want to confuse his students, so he used his remarkable ability to explain complex grammar rules in a simple way.

reflection.definition: n. 反射；映像；倒影；深思，反省；反映
reflection.memory_trick: reflect（反射/思考）的名词形式。镜中的“映像”是光“返回”形成的；内心的“反省”是思想“返回”自身。
reflection.example: His remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly is a reflection of his disciplined study habits and natural aptitude.

prefer.memory_trick: pre-（在前，优先）+ fer（带来）→ 把某物'带到前面' → 更偏爱。
prefer.example: I prefer tea to coffee in the morning.

deform.definition: v. 使变形，使畸形；损坏…的形状
deform.memory_trick: de-（离开，变坏）+ form（形状）→ 形状变坏 → 使变形
deform.example: The accident deformed the metal frame of the car.

transform.definition: v. 使变形，使改观；转换
transform.memory_trick: trans-（跨越，转变）+ form（形状）→ 形状跨越改变 → 变形，转换
transform.example: The new technology has transformed the way we communicate.

translate.memory_trick: trans-（横跨，转移）+ lat-（带来）→ 把意思从一种语言带到另一种语言 → 翻译
translate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him translate complex texts accurately.

disperse.memory_trick: dis-（分开）+ sperse（散开，来自拉丁语 spargere）→ 使分开散开 → 分散
disperse.example: The police arrived to disperse the crowd that had gathered in the square.

dismiss.definition: vt. 解雇，开除；不予考虑，摒弃；解散
dismiss.memory_trick: dis-（离开）+ miss（送，派）→ 送走 → 解雇；解散
dismiss.example: The manager had to dismiss the employee for repeated violations of company policy.

display.definition: vt./n. 展示，陈列；显示，显露
display.memory_trick: dis-（分开）+ play（=plicare，折叠）→ 将折叠的东西展开 → 展示
display.example: The museum will display ancient artifacts from the newly discovered tomb.

distort.definition: vt. 扭曲，使变形；歪曲，曲解
distort.memory_trick: dis-（加强语气）+ tort（扭曲）→ 用力扭曲 → 使变形；歪曲
distort.example: The funhouse mirrors distort your reflection, making you look tall and thin or short and wide.

distract.memory_trick: dis（分开）+ tract（拉）→ 把注意力拉开 → 分心
distract.example: The abnormal weather can distract hikers from their planned route in the mountains.

distribute.definition: vt. 分发，分配；散布，分布
distribute.memory_trick: dis-（分开）+ tribute（给予）→ 分开给 → 分发
distribute.example: Volunteers will distribute food and water to the families affected by the flood.

congress.definition: n. 代表大会；国会，议会
congress.memory_trick: con-(共同) + gress(走，进行) → 大家走到一起开会 → 代表大会，国会。
congress.example: The new policy was debated extensively in congress before being passed.

connect.definition: v. 连接，联结；联系，关联
connect.memory_trick: con-(共同) + nect(绑，结) → 绑在一起 → 连接。
connect.example: The bridge connects the two parts of the city, making travel much easier.

connection.definition: n. 连接，联结；联系，关系
connection.memory_trick: connect(连接)的名词形式 (-ion 表示状态或结果)。
connection.example: There is a clear connection between regular exercise and good health.

retain.memory_trick: re（回，再）+ tain（=tinu，保持）→ 拿回来保持住 → 保留
retain.example: He can retain vocabulary easily, which contributes to his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

sustain.memory_trick: sus（=sub，在下面）+ tain（=tinu，保持）→ 在下面保持住 → 支撑，维持
sustain.example: His motivation sustains his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly over long periods.

reflect.definition: v. 反射（光、热、声等）；反映，映出；深思，反省
reflect.memory_trick: re-（回）+ flect（弯曲）→ 光线或思绪“弯回来”→ 反射或思考。
reflect.example: The calm lake reflected the moonlight, creating a shimmering path across the water.

reject.memory_trick: 前缀 're-' 表示 '回'（back），结合 'ject'（投掷），即 '投掷回去' → 拒绝接受。
reject.example: The committee decided to reject the proposal due to insufficient data.

rejoice.definition: v. 感到高兴，充满喜悦
rejoice.memory_trick: re-（加强语气）+ joice（来自拉丁语'gaudere'，高兴）→ 使非常高兴。
rejoice.example: The whole community rejoiced at the news of the safe return of the missing hikers.

relevant.definition: adj. 相关的，切题的；有重大关系的
relevant.memory_trick: re-（再）+ lev（举起，轻的）+ ant（形容词后缀）→ 再次举起来（引起注意）的 → 相关的，重要的。联想：与主题“相关”的信息会被“再次提起”。
relevant.example: When writing an essay, it's crucial to include only relevant information that directly supports your thesis.

involve.memory_trick: in-（向内）+ volv（滚动）→ 滚入其中 → 卷入、涉及。联想：被“滚动”进某个事件或状态里。
involve.example: Learning a new language quickly may involve immersive practice and frequent interaction with native speakers.

reproach.definition: 1. 动词：责备、指责。2. 名词：耻辱、批评。
reproach.memory_trick: 拆解为 re-（反向）+ proach（接近），原指“反向接近”，引申为“以指责的方式靠近对方”，暗示言语或态度的对抗性接近。
reproach.example: His inability to learn new languages quickly became a source of reproach among his peers.

inflate.definition: v. 使充气；膨胀；抬高（物价）；使骄傲
inflate.memory_trick: 拆解：in-（向内）+ flat（吹气）+ -e → 向内吹气 → 使膨胀。联想：给气球inflate（充气）。
inflate.example: The government's policies may inflate housing prices in the short term.

eject.memory_trick: 前缀 'e-' 表示 '出'（out），结合 'ject'（投掷），即 '投掷出去' → 驱逐或弹出。
eject.example: The pilot had to eject from the malfunctioning aircraft.

inject.memory_trick: 前缀 'in-' 表示 '进入'（into），结合 'ject'（投掷），即 '投掷进去' → 注入或注射。
inject.example: The doctor will inject the vaccine to boost immunity.

project.memory_trick: 前缀 'pro-' 表示 '向前'（forward），结合 'ject'（投掷），即 '向前投掷' → 投射光线、想法或计划。
project.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is crucial for his latest translation project.

compress.memory_trick: com-（共同）+ press（压）→ 一起压 → 压缩
compress.example: He can compress complex ideas into a few concise sentences, showing a remarkable ability to communicate effectively.

depress.memory_trick: de-（向下）+ press（压）→ 向下压 → 使情绪低落
depress.example: Failing to learn new languages quickly might depress him, despite his usual remarkable ability in that area.

express.memory_trick: ex-（出）+ press（压）→ 把思想压出来 → 表达
express.example: He has a remarkable ability to express himself clearly in multiple languages.

impress.memory_trick: im-（进入）+ press（压）→ 压进去 → 印入脑中 → 留下印象
impress.example: His remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly never fails to impress everyone he meets.

oppress.memory_trick: op-（对抗，加强）+ press（压）→ 强行压制 → 压迫
oppress.example: The pressure to master a language quickly should not oppress learners, even those with a remarkable ability.

suppress.memory_trick: sup-（下）+ press（压）→ 压下去 → 抑制
suppress.example: He could not suppress his excitement upon discovering his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

attract.memory_trick: at（加强）+ tract（拉）→ 用力拉过来 → 吸引
attract.example: His remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly can attract many admirers.

contract.memory_trick: con（共同）+ tract（拉）→ 共同拉（约束）→ 合同；或：向内拉 → 收缩
contract.example: The company signed a contract to ensure the project's completion.

extract.memory_trick: ex（出）+ tract（拉）→ 拉出来 → 提取
extract.example: Scientists extract valuable compounds from plants for medical research.

subtract.memory_trick: sub（下）+ tract（拉）→ 拉下去 → 减掉
subtract.example: Remember to subtract the discount from the total price before payment.

stimulate.definition: 刺激；激励；促使；激发（兴趣或活动）
stimulate.memory_trick: 拆解：stimul（刺激）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 使产生刺激作用。联想：老师用问题“刺激”（stimulate）学生思考。
stimulate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which seems to be stimulated by his passion for different cultures.

stimulus.definition: 刺激物；激励因素；促进因素
stimulus.memory_trick: 词根 stimul（刺激）+ us（名词后缀）→ 带来刺激的事物。对比：stimulate（动作），stimulus（事物）。
stimulus.example: The new language environment acted as a powerful stimulus for his rapid learning progress.

stipulate.memory_trick: 拆解为 stipul（约定）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 进行约定 → 规定。联想：合同中的“条款”（stipulation）需要双方共同“规定”（stipulate）。
stipulate.example: The contract stipulates that the project must be completed by December, but the weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, which may affect the schedule.

adapt.memory_trick: 拆解为 ad- (朝向) + apt (适合)。字面意思就是'使之朝向适合的状态'，即'适应'或'调整'。
adapt.example: He can quickly adapt to new environments and learn the local language.

apt.definition: adj. 恰当的；聪明的；有…倾向的
apt.memory_trick: 作为词根本身，直接表示'适合的'。可以联想记忆：一个恰当的（apt）应用程序（app）非常适合（apt）你的需求。
apt.example: He is apt at picking up new skills, such as learning new languages quickly.

aptitude.definition: n. 天资；才能；自然倾向
aptitude.memory_trick: apt (适合，倾向) + -itude (表示状态或性质的名词后缀)。字面意思是'适合做某事的状态'，即'天资'或'才能'。
aptitude.example: He has a remarkable aptitude for learning new languages quickly.

exaggerate.example: He tends to exaggerate his achievements.

appropriate.example: It is appropriate to wear formal clothes to the ceremony.

inferior.example: This product is inferior to the one we bought last time.

overlook.example: Don't overlook the small details in the report.

overseas.example: She plans to study overseas next year.

stir.example: Stir the soup gently to avoid spilling.

stick.example: Please stick the stamp on the envelope.

examine.example: The doctor will examine your eyes.

state.definition: n. 状态；国家；州；情形 v. 陈述；声明
state.memory_trick: 联想：一个国家或州是一个“建立”（sta-）起来的政治实体，其“状态”是固定的。
state.example: The new system runs in a steady state of absolute synchrony.

statue.memory_trick: 雕像是一个“站立”（sta-）着不动的人或物的形象。
statue.example: The bronze statue has stood in the square for over a century.

statistics.memory_trick: 统计学是研究如何“建立”（sta-）和描述社会或数据“状态”的科学。
statistics.example: Statistics show a remarkable ability of the population to learn new languages quickly.

statesman.memory_trick: 政治家是管理“国家”（state）事务的人（man）。
statesman.example: The elder statesman offered a steady hand during the crisis.

status.memory_trick: 直接来自拉丁语status（站立的状态、位置），指一个人或事物“站立”在社会中的位置。
status.example: His status as a leader is steady and unquestioned.

standard.definition: n. 标准；旗帜 adj. 标准的
standard.memory_trick: 标准是“建立”（sta-）起来供大家“站立”（stand）参照的标杆或准则。历史上也指“军旗”（立着的旗帜）。
standard.example: The new system sets a high standard for absolute synchrony.

stance.memory_trick: 指身体“站立”（sta-）的姿势，或思想上所“站立”的立场。
stance.example: He took a firm stance on the issue, standing his ground.

standpoint.memory_trick: 合成词：站立（stand）的点（point），即观察或思考时所“站立”的位置。
standpoint.example: From a technical standpoint, the system's synchrony is remarkable.

stand.definition: v. 站立；忍受；位于 n. 立场；摊位
stand.memory_trick: 词根sta-的直接体现，核心义为“站立”。
stand.example: The old clock has stood in the hall for generations, a steady presence.

statement.memory_trick: 使（-ment）某种观点或事实“站立”（state）出来，即进行陈述。
statement.example: The official statement confirmed the system's steady performance.

statute.memory_trick: 法规是“建立”（sta-）起来让社会“站立”其上的规则，与statue（雕像）同源，都有“确立之物”的含义。
statute.example: The statute provides a steady legal framework for operations.

stay.definition: v. 停留；保持 n. 逗留
stay.memory_trick: 核心义是“持续站立（在某处）”，即停留、保持。
stay.example: The system must stay in absolute synchrony to function properly.

steady.definition: adj. 稳定的；稳固的；持续的 v. 使稳定
steady.memory_trick: 词根sta-（站立）加上形容词后缀-y，表示“处于站立状态的”，即稳固的。
steady.example: He has shown a steady and remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

overall.definition: adj. 全面的，总体的；adv. 总体上，全部地
overall.memory_trick: 拆解为 over（覆盖）+ all（全部）→ 覆盖全部的 → 总体的。联想：一份 '总体' 报告需要 '覆盖所有' 方面。
overall.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which contributes to his overall success in international business.

overlap.definition: v. 重叠，部分相同；n. 重叠部分
overlap.memory_trick: 拆解为 over（在上）+ lap（大腿；重叠部分）→ 一个叠在另一个上面 → 重叠。联想：两片瓦片 '上下重叠' 覆盖屋顶。
overlap.example: His language learning ability and cultural adaptability often overlap, making him highly effective in cross-cultural communication.

overcome.memory_trick: 拆解为 over（超越）+ come（来）→ 超越困难而来 → 克服。联想：翻越（over）障碍物来到（come）目的地。
overcome.example: He overcame the initial difficulties of language learning with consistent practice and now masters multiple languages.

assassinate.memory_trick: 拆解为 ass（驴，笨人）+ assass（谐音“暗杀”）+ inate（动词后缀），联想“笨驴策划的暗杀行动”。结合词根 ass-（朝向）理解：怀着特定目的（如政治、金钱）靠近并杀害目标。
assassinate.example: The plot to assassinate the ambassador was uncovered by intelligence agents.

assert.definition: vt. 断言，主张；维护（权利、权威等）
assert.memory_trick: 拆解为 as（如同）+ sert（加入，插入），联想“如同插入观点般坚持己见”。结合词根 ass-（朝向）理解：将自己的观点或权利“强有力地推向”他人，使其被承认。
assert.example: He asserted his authority by making the final decision on the project.

assemble.definition: v. 集合，聚集；组装，装配
assemble.memory_trick: 拆解为 as（朝向）+ semble（类似，一起），联想“使类似的东西朝一个地方聚集”。结合词根 ass-（朝向）理解：使人或部件“朝一个中心点靠近并组合起来”。
assemble.example: The team will assemble in the conference room to discuss the new strategy.

assault.memory_trick: 谐音“啊！搜他！”，联想突然袭击并搜查。结合词根 ass-（朝向）理解：猛烈地“冲向”目标进行攻击。
assault.example: The soldiers launched a surprise assault on the enemy fortress at dawn.

assembly.definition: n. 集会，集合；装配，组装；立法机构
assembly.memory_trick: assemble（聚集，组装）的名词形式，直接关联。结合词根 ass-（朝向）理解：人或部件“被召集到一起”形成的状态或结果。
assembly.example: The school assembly was held to celebrate the students' achievements.

anxious.definition: adj. 焦虑的，担忧的；渴望的
anxious.memory_trick: 拆解：anx（压迫）+ ious（形容词后缀）→ 内心感到压迫的 → 焦虑的。联想：anxious 发音似“安可歇斯”，但心里不安无法歇息 → 焦虑。
anxious.example: He is anxious about the upcoming exam, but he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

anxiety.memory_trick: 拆解：anx（压迫）+ iety（名词后缀，表状态）→ 内心压迫的状态 → 焦虑。联想：anxiety 结尾 -ety 类似“易惕”，容易警惕不安 → 焦虑感。
anxiety.example: Her anxiety about speaking in public decreased when she realized her remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

anticipate.definition: 1. 预料，预期；2. 预见并提前准备；3. 期盼，期望
anticipate.memory_trick: 拆解：anti-（前，先）+ cip（取，拿，来自拉丁语 capere）+ -ate（动词后缀）→ 提前拿取 → 引申为“预料、预先准备”。联想：anti（前）+ cip（拿）+ ate（吃）→ 提前拿吃的 → 预料到会饿，所以提前准备食物。
anticipate.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, so we should anticipate sudden changes and pack accordingly.

potential.definition: adj. 潜在的，可能的；n. 潜力，潜能
potential.memory_trick: 拆解为 pot（能力）+ ent（形容词后缀）+ ial（形容词后缀），联想为“有能力的状态”→ 潜在的，有潜力的。
potential.example: He has the potential to learn new languages quickly.

possible.definition: adj. 可能的，可能存在的
possible.memory_trick: 拆解为 poss（=pot，能力）+ ible（形容词后缀，表示“可…的”），联想为“有能力做到的”→ 可能的。
possible.example: It is possible for him to learn new languages quickly.

possibility.memory_trick: 由 possible（可能的） + ity（名词后缀）构成，表示“可能的状态或性质”。
possibility.example: There is a strong possibility that he can learn new languages quickly.

narrative.definition: n. 叙述，故事；讲述；adj. 叙述的，叙事体的
narrative.memory_trick: 拆解：narr（讲述）+ ative（形容词/名词后缀）→ 与讲述相关的事物 → 叙述、故事。联想：narr- 音似“那日”，想象“那日发生的故事需要被叙述（narrative）出来”。
narrative.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which became a key part of his personal narrative about adapting to different cultures.

stem.definition: n. 茎，干；柄，梗；词干；血统，家族；船首，船头 v. 起源于，由…造成；阻止，遏制
stem.memory_trick: 联想记忆：植物的“茎干”（stem）是整株植物的“起源”和支撑主干，就像词根 stem- 表示“起源”一样。可以想象一棵树从茎干生长出来，引申为事物的起源或核心部分。
stem.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which stems from his early exposure to multiple cultures.

nation.memory_trick: nat（出生）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 一个由共同“出生”或起源的人们组成的群体 → 民族，国家。
nation.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn the languages of a new nation quickly.

national.definition: adj. 国家的；民族的；国有的 n. 国民
national.memory_trick: nation（国家）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 与国家或民族相关的。
national.example: His national identity gives him a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

nationality.memory_trick: national（国家的）+ ity（名词后缀，表性质）→ 属于某个国家的性质 → 国籍。
nationality.example: His diverse nationality background contributes to his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

native.definition: adj. 本国的；本土的；天生的 n. 本地人；土著
native.memory_trick: nat（出生）+ ive（形容词后缀）→ 与出生地相关的 → 本地的，天生的。
native.example: As a native speaker, he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly by comparison.

nature.memory_trick: nat（出生）+ ure（名词后缀）→ 事物与生俱来的状态 → 天性，本质；引申为孕育万物的“自然”。
nature.example: By nature, he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

natural.definition: adj. 自然的；天生的；不做作的
natural.memory_trick: natur(e)（自然，天性）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 与自然或天性一致的 → 自然的，天生的。
natural.example: It seems natural for him to have a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

composition.definition: n. 组成，构成；作品（尤指音乐或文学作品）；作文
composition.memory_trick: com-（一起）+ pos（放置）+ -ition（名词后缀）→ 将各部分“放置在一起”形成整体 → 构成；作品
composition.example: The composition of the team reflects a balance of experience and fresh talent.

component.definition: n. 组成部分，成分，部件
component.memory_trick: com-（一起）+ pon（放置）+ -ent（名词后缀，表示物）→ 被“放置在一起”构成整体的部分 → 组成部分
component.example: Each component of the device must be tested for reliability.

compose.definition: v. 组成，构成；创作（音乐、诗歌等）；使平静
compose.memory_trick: com-（一起）+ pose（放置）→ 将元素“放置在一起”形成整体 → 组成；创作
compose.example: He can compose a piece of music in just a few hours.

position.definition: n. 位置；职位；立场，观点 v. 安置，放置
position.memory_trick: pos（放置）+ -ition（名词后缀）→ 被放置的地点或状态 → 位置；职位
position.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which puts him in a strong position for the international role.

portion.definition: n. 一部分，一份；（食物的）一客
portion.memory_trick: port（= part，部分，由“放置”引申出的分配概念）+ -ion（名词后缀）→ 被分出来“放置”的一份 → 部分
portion.example: A large portion of his income is spent on books and courses.

comprehend.definition: vt. 理解，领会；包含，包括
comprehend.memory_trick: com-（完全）+ prehend（抓住）→ 完全抓住（信息或内容）→ 理解；包含。可以联想为“将信息完全抓在手中，从而理解”。
comprehend.example: He has a remarkable ability to comprehend new languages quickly.

comprehension.memory_trick: comprehen(d)（理解）+ -sion（名词后缀）→ 理解的行为或能力。注意词根变为 prehens-。
comprehension.example: His quick comprehension of complex grammar rules is impressive.

comprehensive.definition: adj. 全面的，综合的；有理解力的
comprehensive.memory_trick: comprehen(d)（包含，理解）+ -sive（形容词后缀）→ 把所有方面都“包含”进来的 → 全面的；能“理解”广泛的 → 有理解力的。
comprehensive.example: He conducted a comprehensive study of the language's history and structure.

disappear.memory_trick: dis-（分离）+ appear（出现）→ 从出现状态分离 → 消失
disappear.example: The magician made the rabbit disappear in an instant.

discovery.definition: n. 发现，被发现的事物
discovery.memory_trick: dis-（否定）+ cover（覆盖）+ -y（名词后缀）→ 不再覆盖 → 发现
discovery.example: The discovery of penicillin revolutionized medicine.

discover.memory_trick: dis-（否定）+ cover（覆盖）→ 揭开覆盖 → 发现
discover.example: Scientists continue to discover new species in the rainforest.

disclose.memory_trick: dis-（否定）+ close（关闭）→ 不再关闭 → 公开
disclose.example: The company refused to disclose its financial details.

disable.definition: vt. 使失去能力，使无效
disable.memory_trick: dis-（否定）+ able（能够）→ 使不能 → 使失效
disable.example: The virus can disable the computer's security system.

disappoint.memory_trick: dis-（否定）+ appoint（任命）→ 未被任命 → 失望（引申义）
disappoint.example: I hope the results won't disappoint you.

discipline.definition: n. 纪律；学科 vt. 训练，管教
discipline.memory_trick: dis-（分离）+ cip（拿）+ line（线）→ 拿线分开 → 有界限 → 纪律
discipline.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which requires great discipline.

condemn.memory_trick: 拆解：cond-（加强）+ -emn（来自拉丁语 damnare，意为谴责）→ 强烈谴责。联想：共同（cond-）给予严厉判决。
condemn.example: The international community was quick to condemn the unjust actions.

condense.memory_trick: 拆解：cond-（加强）+ -dense（密集的）→ 使更加密集。联想：共同压缩使内容更紧凑。
condense.example: He has a remarkable ability to condense complex ideas into clear summaries quickly.

condition.memory_trick: 拆解：cond-（共同）+ -ition（名词后缀）→ 共同约定的条款。联想：共同设定的前提或状态。
condition.example: His ability to learn new languages quickly is conditioned by consistent practice.

conduct.memory_trick: 拆解：cond-（加强）+ -duct（引导）→ 强力引导或带领。联想：共同引导一个过程或行为。
conduct.example: She will conduct a study on how people learn new languages quickly.

conductor.memory_trick: 拆解：conduct（引导）+ -or（人/物）→ 引导者或引导物。联想：共同引导电流、音乐或乘客的人。
conductor.example: The conductor's remarkable ability to coordinate the orchestra impressed everyone.

holder.definition: n. 持有者；支撑物；容器
holder.memory_trick: hold（持有）+ -er（表示人或物的后缀）→ 持有东西的人或物。联想：cup holder（杯架）就是 hold cups 的东西。
holder.example: He is the current holder of the language learning record.

hold.definition: v. 持有；保持；容纳 n. 抓握；控制
hold.memory_trick: 词根本身。想象用手 hold 住一个东西的基本动作。
hold.example: He has a remarkable ability to hold new language knowledge in his mind quickly.

household.definition: n. 家庭；户 adj. 家用的；家庭的
household.memory_trick: house（房子）+ hold（维持）→ 一个房子所维持的基本单位，即“家庭”。联想：一个家庭共同 hold 着一个家。
household.example: In his household, the ability to learn new languages quickly is highly valued.

however.definition: adv. 然而；无论如何 conj. 不管怎样
however.memory_trick: how（如何）+ ever（在任何时候）→ 原意“无论以何种方式”，引申为转折“然而”。注意与 hold 的关联较弱，但词源上 'how' 与古英语 'hū'（方式）相关，此处列出作为包含该词形的单词。
however.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly; however, consistent practice is still essential.

startle.definition: v. 使惊吓，使吃惊；n. 惊跳，吃惊
startle.memory_trick: 拆解为 start（开始）+ le（表反复动作）→ 突然开始的动作 → 惊吓。联想：突然的响动让人“惊跳起来”（startle）。
startle.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which never fails to startle his colleagues.

start.definition: v. 开始，启动；惊起，突然出现；n. 开始，开端；惊跳
start.memory_trick: 词根本身即“突然移动”，引申为“突然开始”。可联想赛跑时发令枪响，选手“突然启动”（start）。
start.example: He has a remarkable ability to start learning new languages quickly and make progress from day one.

steer.definition: 及物动词：驾驶（车、船等）；引导，操纵；不及物动词：驾驶；行驶；名词：阉牛（此义项与词根无关，属同形异义）。
steer.memory_trick: 联想记忆：steer（驾驶）→ 司机（driver）steer the car（开车）；谐音“驶舵”→ 操控方向舵即“驾驶”。
steer.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him steer through multicultural environments with ease.

hoist.definition: 动词：升起，吊起，提升；名词：起重机，升降设备
hoist.memory_trick: 联想记忆：hoist 发音类似“嗨，升起来！”——在工地或码头，人们常喊“hoist!”指挥起重，声音响亮有力，便于记忆其“升起”的动作场景。
hoist.example: The workers used a crane to hoist the heavy equipment to the top of the building.

apart.definition: adv. 分开地；相隔；拆散；区别地；单独地
apart.memory_trick: 拆解为 a（离开）+ part（部分）→ 分成部分 → 分开。联想：将物体“a part”（一个部分）拆开，就是 apart（分开）。
apart.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, setting him apart from his peers.

starve.definition: 1. (使) 饿死；(因缺乏食物而) 死亡；2. (使) 极度缺乏；(使) 渴望得到某物
starve.memory_trick: 联想记忆：star（星星）+ ve（谐音“饿”）→ 饿得眼冒金星 → 饿死。或拆分：star（明星）+ ve（谐音“无”）→ 明星无食物 → 饿死。
starve.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, and hikers may starve if they are not prepared with enough food.

nasty.definition: 1. 肮脏的，污秽的；令人作呕的。 2. 令人不快的，讨厌的；恶劣的（如天气）。 3. 恶意的，恶毒的；下流的。 4. 严重的，危险的（如伤势或问题）。
nasty.memory_trick: 联想记忆：'nasty' 听起来像 '那死地'，可以想象一个又脏又臭、令人不快的地方，帮助记忆其'肮脏、令人不快'的核心含义。
nasty.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a nasty surprise for his competitors who thought they had an advantage.

narrow.definition: 1. 狭窄的，窄的（物理空间）；2. 狭隘的，有限的（范围、程度或视野）；3. 勉强的，相差微小的；4. 动词：使变窄，缩小
narrow.memory_trick: 联想记忆：'narrow' 发音类似“那肉”，想象一块肉被切得非常窄的条状，引申为“狭窄”。或拆解：'nar'（谐音“那”）+ 'row'（排）→ “那一排很窄”。
narrow.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, despite his narrow exposure to different cultures.

step.definition: n. 脚步；步骤；台阶；阶段；措施 v. 迈步；行走；踩
step.memory_trick: 联想记忆：'step' 发音类似“踏步”，直接对应“脚步”或“步骤”的动作与概念。
step.example: He has taken a significant step forward in his ability to learn new languages quickly.

pour.definition: 1. 使（液体）连续流出；倒；灌；注。 2. （雨）倾盆而下；下倾盆大雨。 3. （人）大量涌入（或涌出）。 4. 倾吐，倾诉（感情等）。
pour.memory_trick: 联想记忆：想象“泼（pōu）水”的动作，发音相似，动作也相似，都是将液体倒出。
pour.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, as if knowledge pours into his mind effortlessly.

huddle.definition: v. 挤作一团；蜷缩；聚集商议 n. 杂乱的一团；小型秘密会议
huddle.memory_trick: 联想：hu（音似“互”）+ ddle（音似“兜”）→ 互相兜在一起 → 挤作一团。
huddle.example: The team huddled together to discuss the final strategy before the game.

answer.definition: n. 回答，答复；答案；解决办法 v. 回答，答复；回应；符合，满足
answer.memory_trick: 联想：'an'（一个）+ 'swer'（谐音'思我'）→ 一个问题需要一个'思我'（思考后的我）来'回答'。
answer.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he can answer complex questions in them within months.

anonymous.example: The donor wished to remain anonymous.

stern.example: The teacher gave him a stern look.

distinct.example: There is a distinct difference between the two ideas.

fruitful.example: The meeting was fruitful and led to many new ideas.

distinction.example: She made a clear distinction between right and wrong.

mood.example: He is in a good mood today.

transmission.example: The transmission of the disease can be prevented.

stock.example: The company's stock has increased in value.

transmit.example: The radio station transmits signals 24 hours a day.

stun.example: The news stunned everyone in the room.

monotonous.example: The work is monotonous and boring.

prime.example: This is a prime example of modern art.

evidence.example: The police found evidence at the crime scene.

evil.example: He is known for his evil deeds.

enterprise.example: She started her own enterprise last year.

entertainment.example: The city offers many forms of entertainment.

licens(e).example: You need a license to drive a car.

primary.example: The primary goal is to finish the project on time.

liable.example: He is liable for the damages.

strike.example: The workers went on strike for better pay.

fund.example: They raised funds for the charity.

store.example: I need to go to the store to buy groceries.

constrain.example: Lack of funds constrained the project.

transport.example: Public transport is convenient in this city.

structure.example: The building has a strong structure.

constant.example: The machine runs at a constant speed.

principal.example: The principal reason is the cost.

function.example: The function of the heart is to pump blood.

stride.example: She took a long stride forward.

liberal.example: He holds liberal views on education.

construct.example: They plan to construct a new bridge.

fundamental.example: This is a fundamental principle of physics.

primitive.example: Primitive tools were made of stone.

monopoly.example: The company has a monopoly on the market.

amend.example: The law was amended last year.

evident.example: It is evident that he is lying.

amount.example: The amount of money is insufficient.

innocent.example: The child is innocent of the crime.

ample.example: There is ample space in the room.

among(st).example: She is among the best students.

consistent.example: His actions are consistent with his words.

constituent.example: The constituents voted for the new law.

constitution.example: The constitution guarantees freedom of speech.

string.example: She tied the package with a string.

console.example: He tried to console her after the loss.

consist.example: The team consists of five members.

striking.example: She has a striking resemblance to her mother.

transplant.example: The patient received a heart transplant.

distinguish.example: Can you distinguish between the two colors?

per.memory_trick: per 就是词根本身，记住“通过每个”即可。

percent.memory_trick: per（每）+ cent（一百）= 每一百中的数量，即百分比。
percent.example: Only 20 percent of the students passed the exam.

percentage.memory_trick: percent + age（名词后缀），表示百分比的概念或数值。
percentage.example: The percentage of women in the workforce has increased.

perceive.memory_trick: per（完全）+ ceive（拿取）= 完全拿取信息，即感知或理解。
perceive.example: He has a remarkable ability to perceive subtle changes in people's moods.

perfect.memory_trick: per（完全）+ fect（做）= 做得完全彻底，即完美的。
perfect.example: She spent years perfecting her craft.

operator.memory_trick: 'operate'（操作）+ 'or'（人）→ 执行操作的人。想象电话‘接线员’在‘工作’中快速切换线路。
operator.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which would make him an excellent operator in a multilingual call center.

operate.memory_trick: 词根'oper'（工作）+ 'ate'（动词后缀）→ 做工作 → 操作。想象医生‘工作’在手术台上。
operate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him operate effectively in international teams.

operation.memory_trick: 来自'operate'（操作）+ 'tion'（名词后缀）→ 操作的过程或结果。联想一次精密的‘手术’需要团队协作的‘工作’。
operation.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, making him invaluable for complex operations abroad.

operational.definition: 操作的；运营的；可使用的
operational.memory_trick: 'operation'（操作）+ 'al'（形容词后缀）→ 与操作相关的。联想一个‘运营’团队不断‘工作’使系统保持可用状态。
operational.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, ensuring the team remains operational in diverse regions.

opportunity.memory_trick: op（朝向）+ port（港口）+ unity（状态）= 朝向港口的状态，即有利时机。
opportunity.example: He has a remarkable ability to seize every opportunity.

opposite.memory_trick: op（相反）+ pos（放置）+ ite（形容词后缀）= 放在对面的，即相反的。
opposite.example: They sat on opposite sides of the table.

oppose.memory_trick: op（相反）+ pose（放置）= 放在对立位置，即反对。
oppose.example: Many citizens oppose the new policy.

opponent.memory_trick: op（相反）+ pon（放置）+ ent（人）= 站在对面的人，即对手。
opponent.example: He defeated his opponent in the final match.

oppress.memory_trick: op（加强）+ press（压）= 用力压，即压迫。
oppress.example: The regime oppressed its citizens for decades.

peer.definition: n. 同辈，同龄人；贵族；v. 凝视，仔细看
peer.memory_trick: 平等(par)的观看(peer)——同辈之间相互凝视，地位相当。
peer.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, often surprising his peers.

pare.memory_trick: 使平等(par)——削去多余部分让事物变得整齐对等，如削苹果皮。
pare.example: He has a remarkable ability to pare down complex ideas into simple explanations.

compare.memory_trick: 共同(com-)放在平等(par)的位置——放在一起比较异同。
compare.example: He has a remarkable ability to compare different language structures and find patterns.

appear.memory_trick: 向(ad-)观看(peer)——使某物被看到，即出现。
appear.example: He has a remarkable ability to appear confident even in challenging situations.

peculiar.definition: 奇怪的，独特的；私人的，特有的
peculiar.memory_trick: pecu（私人财产）+ liar（说谎者）→ 私人财产被说谎者盯上，情况变得‘奇怪’而‘独特’。
peculiar.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite peculiar this time of year.

pecuniary.memory_trick: pecu（私人财产）+ niary（联想‘货币’）→ 私人财产和货币相关，即‘金钱的’。
pecuniary.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he faces pecuniary difficulties in pursuing his studies.

maximum.definition: n. 最大量，最大限度；adj. 最大的，最高的
maximum.memory_trick: max（最大）+ imum（拉丁语最高级后缀）→ 最大值。想象一个温度计上升到顶端刻度“MAX”，就是maximum。
maximum.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

maximize.definition: v. 使最大化，充分利用
maximize.memory_trick: max（最大）+ imize（动词后缀，表示“使成为”）→ 使达到最大。联想：把音量按钮调到“MAX”档，就是maximize音量。
maximize.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

pay.memory_trick: 想象你付钱（pay）时，对方露出和平（pac）的微笑，因为钱能安抚一切。
pay.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he must pay for the courses.

payment.memory_trick: 支付（pay）+ 名词后缀-ment = 付款的行为或金额，记住：每次支付都是对和平（pac）的承诺。
payment.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he made the payment for the online course.

peace.memory_trick: 发音类似 'piece'，想象一片（piece）宁静的和平（peace）之地，没有纷争。
peace.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which brings him inner peace.

mean.definition: v. 意思是；打算；导致 adj. 吝啬的；平均的；中间的 n. 平均值；中间
mean.memory_trick: 想象一个天平：中间指针指在‘平均’刻度上，两边分别放着‘吝啬’和‘慷慨’的砝码——指针就是 mean。
mean.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly. (此句不含mean，但可替换为：What does this word mean in this context?)

meaning.memory_trick: mean（意味）+ ing（名词后缀）→ 意味的结果就是‘意义’。
meaning.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the meaning of this sentence is clear. (他快速学习新语言的能力非凡，但这句话的意思是清楚的。)

meantime.definition: n. 其间；同时 adv. 与此同时
meantime.memory_trick: ‘mean’（中间）+‘time’（时间）→ 两段事件之间的时间，即‘在此期间’。
meantime.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly; in the meantime, he also masters several dialects.

meanwhile.definition: adv. 与此同时；在此期间
meanwhile.memory_trick: mean（中间）+ while（时间段）→ 在中间的这段时间里→ ‘与此同时’。
meanwhile.example: He studied hard; meanwhile, his brother played video games. (他努力学习；与此同时，他的兄弟在玩电子游戏。)

means.definition: n. 方法；手段；收入（复数形式常作单数用）
means.memory_trick: ‘mean’加上‘s’变成复数，就像‘方法’不止一种——中间有多种途径达到目标。
means.example: We must abolish the outdated regulations. (此句不含means，可替换为：We must find effective means to solve this problem.)

worth.memory_trick: 与 'worship' 同源，去掉 'ship' 保留核心‘价值’，记住‘有 worth 才值得 worship’。
worth.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and that skill is worth a lot in the global market.

worthwhile.memory_trick: 拆分记忆：worth（价值）+ while（一段时间）→ 花时间有价值 → 值得做的。
worthwhile.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, making every study session worthwhile.

worthy.definition: 值得的；应得的；有价值的
worthy.memory_trick: 联想‘worthy = worth + y（形容词后缀）’，直接记忆为‘有价值的、值得的’。
worthy.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he is worthy of praise.

write.memory_trick: 想象用手（right hand）在纸上写（write）字，发音同 'right'，但拼写不同，记住 'w' 像笔尖。
write.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he often writes down new words to remember them.

writer.memory_trick: write + er（表示人），即 '写字的人'，联想到职业作家。
writer.example: The writer spent years perfecting his novel, showing a remarkable ability to learn new writing techniques quickly.

script.definition: 脚本；手稿；笔迹；文字系统
script.memory_trick: 来自 'scrib' 的过去分词，想象剧本（script）是写好的文字，演员照着念。
script.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he can even read scripts in those languages.

describe.memory_trick: de（向下）+ scribe（写），意为 '写下来'，引申为用语言详细描绘。
describe.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he can describe complex ideas with ease.

scope.definition: n. 范围；视野；机会；v. 仔细看，审视
scope.memory_trick: scop（看）+ e → 眼睛能看到的地方就是“范围”。
scope.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which broadens the scope of his career opportunities.

microscope.memory_trick: micro（小）+ scope（看）→ 用来观察微小物体的仪器。
microscope.example: Under the microscope, the scientist observed tiny organisms that were invisible to the naked eye.

telescope.memory_trick: tele（远）+ scope（看）→ 用来观察远处物体的仪器。
telescope.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, much like a telescope allows one to see distant stars clearly.

rank.definition: n. 等级，军衔；排，行；v. 排列，分等级；adj. 茂密的，难闻的
rank.memory_trick: 想象军队列队（rank）时，按照军衔（rank）高低排列，高官站在前排。
rank.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which ranks him among the top linguists in the field.

range.definition: n. 范围，幅度；山脉；v. 排列，（在一定范围内）变化
range.memory_trick: 山脉（range）像一排排（rank）连绵起伏的线条，延伸出广阔的范围（range）。
range.example: The mountains range from 1,000 to 3,000 meters in height, offering a wide range of climbing challenges.

arrange.memory_trick: 前缀ar-（加强）+ range（排列）→ 把东西排整齐，就是‘安排’好一切。
arrange.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he arranged his study schedule to practice three languages daily.

urgent.memory_trick: urg（催促）+ ent（形容词后缀）→ 需要催促的 → 紧急的。想象一个不断催促你完成的任务，就是urgent。
urgent.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but this urgent deadline requires immediate action.

urge.definition: 强烈欲望；冲动；催促；驱策
urge.memory_trick: 直接来自词根urg，意为‘驱使’→ 内心被驱使的感觉 → 强烈冲动。想象一股力量推着你去做某事。
urge.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he felt a strong urge to master another one.

through.definition: prep. 穿过，通过；adv. 彻底地，自始至终
through.memory_trick: 想象一根针穿过（through）布料，thr-就像针尖的穿透动作。
through.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he works through every challenge with determination.

throughout.definition: prep. 遍及，贯穿；adv. 到处，始终
throughout.memory_trick: through（穿过）+ out（出去）= 从一头穿到另一头再出去，即“遍及整个范围”。
throughout.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his enthusiasm remains throughout the entire course.

thrive.definition: v. 茁壮成长，兴旺发达
thrive.memory_trick: thr-（穿过）+ -ive（动词后缀）= 生命之力穿透阻碍，蓬勃生长。
thrive.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he thrives in multilingual environments.

thrill.definition: n. 激动，震颤；v. 使兴奋，使震颤
thrill.memory_trick: thr-（穿过）+ -ill（像ill疾病）= 一种穿透身体的强烈感觉，既可能是激动也可能是恐惧。
thrill.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and the thrill of mastering a new dialect keeps him motivated.

thrift.memory_trick: thr-（穿过）+ -ift（如gift礼物）= 让每一分钱都“穿过”最有效的用途，不浪费。
thrift.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his thrift allows him to fund his language courses.

pattern.memory_trick: 想象父亲（patr）是孩子模仿的‘模式’和‘典范’，他的一举一动都成为可复制的‘图案’。
pattern.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, following a pattern of consistent practice.

patron.memory_trick: 父亲（patr）常是家庭的经济支持者，就像赞助人（patron）资助艺术家或老主顾照顾生意。
patron.example: The wealthy patron sponsored the artist's exhibition.

patent.memory_trick: 父亲（patr）发明了独特的方法，申请‘专利’（patent）来保护自己的成果，就像保护自己的‘模式’不被抄袭。
patent.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a patent advantage in his career.

wonder.definition: 惊奇，奇迹；感到惊讶，想知道
wonder.memory_trick: 联想‘wander’（漫游）——在漫游中看到奇景就会‘wonder’（惊叹）。
wonder.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

wonderful.memory_trick: wonder（奇迹）+ ful（充满）→ 充满奇迹的 → 极好的。
wonderful.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

opera.memory_trick: 想象一部歌剧是许多音乐家合作的‘作品’（opus），需要大量‘工作’（opera）排练。
opera.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, just as a conductor learns an opera score.

masterpiece.memory_trick: ‘大师’（master）的‘作品’（piece）就是‘杰作’（masterpiece），好比达芬奇的《蒙娜丽莎》。
masterpiece.example: The painting is considered a masterpiece of Renaissance art.

mate.memory_trick: 想象你和最好的朋友一起下棋，你们是棋友（mate），互相配合。
mate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his study mate often benefits from his help.

material.definition: 材料；原料；物质的；重要的
material.memory_trick: 材料（material）是制作物品的'同伴'，没有它，任何东西都无法成形。
material.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he often uses various learning materials to practice.

mature.definition: 成熟的；到期的；充分发展的
mature.memory_trick: 成熟的（mature）果实就像找到了'同伴'——时间与养分，共同成就了饱满的状态。
mature.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, showing a mature understanding of grammar rules.

mathematical.memory_trick: 数学（mathematical）需要数字和公式像'同伴'一样紧密协作，才能得出精确结果。
mathematical.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, approaching patterns with a mathematical precision.

secure.definition: adj. 安全的；牢固的；有把握的 v. 使安全；获得；固定
secure.memory_trick: 谐音“西克尤尔”，联想成“西边克服了忧虑”，从而感到安全。
secure.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes his future career very secure.

security.definition: n. 安全；安保；保障；抵押品
security.memory_trick: secure（安全）+ ity（名词后缀）→ 安全的状态或系统。想象一个“安全门”上写着“security”。
security.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which gives him a sense of job security.

cure.definition: v. 治愈；治疗；解决 n. 疗法；药物
cure.memory_trick: 谐音“可忧”，但实际是“消除忧虑”——治愈疾病让人不再担忧。
cure.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but there is no quick cure for a broken heart.

tip.memory_trick: 想象一个‘尖’（tip）的铅笔头，轻轻一‘点’（tip）就能给出‘提示’（tip）。
tip.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he always shares useful tips with his classmates.

top.memory_trick: ‘Top’像‘塔顶’的‘顶’（top），想象一座塔的‘顶部’（top）是最高点。
top.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he always ranks at the top of his class.

score.definition: n. 得分；分数；刻痕；乐谱 v. 得分；刻划；记分
score.memory_trick: 想象在木板上用刀刻下一道道痕迹（score），每刻一道代表得一分，如同比赛记分板上的刻痕。
score.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, scoring high on every test.

scorn.definition: n. 轻蔑；鄙视 v. 轻蔑；不屑（做某事）
scorn.memory_trick: 用刀（sc-）在心上划出刻痕（-orn），表示对某人或某事的极度鄙视，如同在心上留下轻蔑的伤疤。
scorn.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he scorns those who struggle with even basic phrases.

utilize.definition: 利用；使用（尤指为实际目的而有效使用）
utilize.memory_trick: util（使用）+ ize（动词后缀）→ 使有用 → 利用。联想：把闲置资源‘实用化’（utilize）就是让它变得有用。
utilize.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he knows how to utilize every resource available to him.

utilisation.definition: 利用；使用（强调过程或状态）
utilisation.memory_trick: util（使用）+ isation（名词后缀）→ 使用的过程。记忆：‘利用’（utilisation）就是让东西‘有用起来’的动作。
utilisation.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, so the utilisation of proper gear is essential for safety.

utility.memory_trick: util（有用）+ ity（名词后缀）→ 有用性。联想：一个东西的‘效用’（utility）就是它‘有多有用’。
utility.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is of great utility in his international career.

master.definition: 主人；大师；硕士；精通，掌握
master.memory_trick: 想象一位‘大师’（master）在‘掌控’（master）全局，就像魔术师挥动魔杖。
master.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and aims to master three more this year.

rail.definition: n. 轨道；栏杆；铁轨 v. 抱怨；责骂
rail.memory_trick: 想象火车在笔直的‘轨道’上行驶，你扶着‘栏杆’站稳，同时有人因火车晚点而‘抱怨’（rail against）。
rail.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

railroad.definition: n. 铁路；铁道部门 v. 迫使…仓促行事；草率通过
railroad.memory_trick: rail（轨道）+ road（路）= 用铁轨铺成的路，即‘铁路’。联想：铁路公司常‘迫使’立法机构快速通过法案。
railroad.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year.

railway.definition: n. 铁路；铁道（英式英语常用）
railway.memory_trick: rail（轨道）+ way（道路）= 轨道之路，即‘铁路’。与railroad同义，但railway更强调轨道系统本身。
railway.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

rally.definition: 召集；集合；恢复；重新振作；汽车拉力赛
rally.memory_trick: 想象一群人被召集（rally）在一起，大家举起手（raise）欢呼，场面热闹，rally = raise + ally（盟友），盟友一起举起手。
rally.example: Despite the setback, the team managed to rally and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helped them communicate with international partners.

patch.definition: n. 补丁；小块；眼罩；斑点；v. 修补；打补丁
patch.memory_trick: 想象一块布片（patch）盖在衣服破洞上，就像给衣服打了一个‘补丁’；或联想到电脑‘补丁’程序，用来修复漏洞。
patch.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still needs to patch the gaps in his grammar.

dispatch.definition: v. 派遣；发送；迅速处理；n. 急件；派遣；迅速处理
dispatch.memory_trick: 前缀 dis-（分开、离开）+ patch（片、补丁）→ 把‘片’（消息或任务）‘分开送走’→ 派遣、发送。想象将一封封‘急件’像补丁一样快速‘发送’出去。
dispatch.example: The manager had to dispatch the urgent orders with great speed, much like his ability to learn new languages quickly.

title.definition: 标题；头衔；称号；所有权凭证
title.memory_trick: 联想‘tie它’——用标题把文章内容‘系’在一起，或头衔把身份‘系’住。
title.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

entitle.definition: 给……定名；使有资格；给……称号
entitle.memory_trick: en-（使）+ title（标题/权利）→ 使拥有标题或权利，即‘定名’或‘使有资格’。
entitle.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

urban.definition: adj. 城市的，都市的；住在都市的
urban.memory_trick: urb（城市）+ an（形容词后缀）→ 城市的。想象‘城市里的公交（urban bus）’帮助记忆。
urban.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is essential in an urban environment with diverse cultures.

suburb.definition: n. 郊区，城郊；近郊住宅区
suburb.memory_trick: sub（在…之下，靠近）+ urb（城市）→ 靠近城市的地方 → 郊区。想象‘地铁（subway）从城市开往郊区（suburb）’。
suburb.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helped him adapt to life in the suburb where many immigrants settled.

see.memory_trick: 想象‘see’就像用眼睛‘C’形扫描，看到一切。
see.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and I see great potential in him.

seek.memory_trick: ‘seek’和‘see’相似，但多了‘k’，像‘看’到目标后‘刻’意去追。
seek.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he will seek every opportunity to practice.

seem.memory_trick: ‘seem’像‘see’加‘m’，‘看’到模糊的‘模样’，所以是‘似乎’。
seem.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which may seem effortless to others.

seemingly.memory_trick: ‘seem’加‘ingly’，像‘似乎’的副词形式，表示‘表面上看’。
seemingly.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, seemingly without any struggle.

random.definition: adj. 随机的，任意的；n. 随机；随意
random.memory_trick: 联想'run'（奔跑）+ 'dom'（领域）→ 在领域内乱跑，毫无规律 → 随机
random.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his study schedule is completely random.

randomness.definition: n. 随机性，偶然性；无规律的状态
randomness.memory_trick: random（随机）+ ness（名词后缀）→ 随机性；也可以记作'乱（ran）动（dom）的状态'
randomness.example: The randomness of the data made it difficult to identify any pattern.

utter.definition: 完全的，彻底的；发出声音，说出
utter.memory_trick: 联想‘out-er’（向外），完全从内向外表达或彻底显现。
utter.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is an utter surprise to everyone.

utterly.memory_trick: utter + ly = 副词形式，表示‘完全地’，如‘utterly impossible’（完全不可能）。
utterly.example: He was utterly convinced that he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

worship.memory_trick: 联想 'worth'（价值）+ 'ship'（身份/状态），认为某物有极高价值而给予敬仰身份。
worship.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he never worships his own talent.

raise.definition: 举起；提高；增加；筹集；引起
raise.memory_trick: 想象一个人举起（raise）双手，手掌向上托举，动作干脆有力，就像在说“raise”时嘴巴张大向上提拉。
raise.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which raises his confidence in international meetings.

rise.definition: 上升；升起；增加；起床；起义
rise.memory_trick: 太阳每天自动升起（rise），无需外力，记住“rise”是不及物动词，自己往上走，字母‘i’像一个人站立挺直。
rise.example: The sun will rise at 6:30 tomorrow morning, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, waking up early to study.

utilize/utilise.example: We must utilize all available resources.

railroad/railway.example: The railroad connects the two cities.

opening.example: The opening of the store attracted many customers.

opinion.example: In my opinion, this is the best solution.

measure.example: We need to measure the length of the room.

accessory.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ cess（走）+ ory（名词后缀）→ 跟着主要物品走的东西 → 附件
accessory.example: He bought several car accessories, including a phone holder and a dash cam.

acceptance.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ cept（拿）+ ance（名词后缀）→ 伸手去拿 → 接受
acceptance.example: His acceptance of the award was met with thunderous applause.

accept.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ cept（拿）→ 伸手去拿 → 接受
accept.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

access.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ cess（走）→ 走向某处 → 进入
access.example: Students have free access to the library's online resources.

accelerate.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ celer（快）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 使朝向快 → 加速
accelerate.example: The government plans to accelerate the construction of new highways.

field.definition: n. 田，地；牧场；（知识或活动的）领域，范围；场地，场所；v. 接（球），接防；处理，应付（问题或意见）
field.memory_trick: 想象一片开阔的田野（field），你可以在这里自由奔跑，就像在某个知识领域（field）里自由探索。
field.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which gives him an advantage in the field of linguistics.

fierce.definition: adj. 激烈的；凶猛的；强烈的；狂热的
fierce.memory_trick: 想象一头野生的狮子（fer-）在咆哮，它的眼神‘fierce’（凶猛）得让人不敢靠近。
fierce.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

change.memory_trick: cambi- 改变 → change 发音类似'趁之'，趁势改变自己。
change.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

chance.memory_trick: 改变(cambi)带来机会(chance)：人生改变往往源于一次偶然的机遇。
chance.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

channel.memory_trick: 改变(cambi)的通道(channel)：要改变现状，需要找到正确的渠道。
channel.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

challenge.memory_trick: 改变(cambi)带来挑战(challenge)：每一次改变都伴随着新的挑战。
challenge.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

threat.memory_trick: threat = th（think思考）+reat（great大）→ 想到一个大坏蛋在威胁你
threat.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his reckless behavior poses a threat to the team's safety.

threaten.definition: v. 威胁；恐吓；预示危险
threaten.memory_trick: threat（威胁）+ en（使动词后缀）→ 使…受到威胁，即‘威胁’的动作
threaten.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his constant lateness threatens to undermine the project's deadline.

begin.memory_trick: 联想：be（是）+ gin（开始）→ “就是开始”，直接对应词根含义。
begin.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he will begin studying Japanese next month.

beginning.memory_trick: begin + ning（动名词后缀）→ 将“开始”名词化，意为“开始的状态或时间点”。
beginning.example: The beginning of his language learning journey was challenging, but he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

belief.memory_trick: bel-（相信）+ -ief（名词后缀）→ 相信的状态或事物。可以联想‘b’像一个人张开双臂拥抱真理，'elief'谐音‘一来福’→ 相信能带来福气。
belief.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

believe.memory_trick: bel-（相信）+ -ieve（动词后缀）→ 去相信。可以拆解为‘be + lie + ve’：be（是）+ lie（谎言）→ 即使听起来像谎言，也选择‘是’（相信），ve代表‘验证’后依然相信。
believe.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

withdraw.memory_trick: with（向后）+ draw（拉）→ 向后拉 → 撤回、退出
withdraw.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he decided to withdraw from the advanced class to focus on basics.

incline.definition: v. 倾斜；倾向（于）；n. 斜面，斜坡
incline.memory_trick: in-（进入）+ clin-（倾斜）= 向里倾斜，即倾向于某方向
incline.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes me incline to believe he is a linguistic genius.

global.memory_trick: glob（球）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 像球一样覆盖整个地球 → 全球的
global.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a global asset in today's interconnected world.

globe.memory_trick: glob（球）+ e（名词后缀）→ 直接指球体或地球
globe.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he dreams of traveling around the globe to use them.

witness.definition: n. 目击者，证人；v. 目击，见证
witness.memory_trick: wit（知道）+ ness（名词后缀）→ 亲眼“知道”事件的人 → 目击者
witness.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, as witnessed by his fluency in five languages.

wit.definition: n. 智慧，才智；机智，风趣
wit.memory_trick: 直接来自词根 wit-，知道得多自然有“智慧”，反应快则显“机智”
wit.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a wit that impresses everyone.

first.definition: 第一的；最初的；首要的；最先
first.memory_trick: 想象‘first’就像‘fast’（快速）的变体——跑得最快的人就是第一名。
first.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

belong.definition: v. 属于；应被放置；是……的成员
belong.memory_trick: be（是）+ long（长）→ 长久地是某物的一部分 → 属于
belong.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

deliberate.definition: adj. 故意的；深思熟虑的；从容的。v. 仔细考虑；商议。
deliberate.memory_trick: de（加强）+ liber（权衡）+ ate（动词/形容词后缀）= 反复权衡，即深思熟虑；故意为之也是经过权衡的结果。
deliberate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

fit.definition: v. 适合，合身；adj. 合适的，健康的
fit.memory_trick: 想象一件衣服（fit）正好合身，就像拼图（fit）完美匹配。
fit.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but this method doesn't fit his learning style.

fitting.definition: adj. 适合的，恰当的；n. 配件，试穿
fitting.memory_trick: fit（适合）+ ing（进行时）→ 正在使东西合适的过程，比如试穿衣服或安装配件。
fitting.example: It is fitting that he should receive the award for his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

win.definition: 赢得；获胜；在竞争、比赛或努力中取得胜利或获得某物
win.memory_trick: 想象你在比赛中挥动拳头（win 的发音像“win”），大声喊“我赢了！”——每次看到 win 就联想到胜利的欢呼。
win.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

update.definition: 更新；使现代化；向…提供最新信息
update.memory_trick: up（向上）+ date（日期）→ 把日期向上推 → 更新到最新状态
update.example: He has a remarkable ability to update his knowledge quickly.

upgrade.definition: 升级；提升；提高（质量、等级等）
upgrade.memory_trick: up（向上）+ grade（等级）→ 等级向上提升 → 升级
upgrade.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, so hikers should upgrade their gear.

up-to-date.definition: 最新的；现代的；包含最新信息的
up-to-date.memory_trick: up（向上）+ to（到达）+ date（日期）→ 向上到达最新日期 → 紧跟时代
up-to-date.example: He has a remarkable ability to stay up-to-date with new languages.

upset.definition: 使心烦意乱；打乱；推翻；不适的
upset.memory_trick: up（向上）+ set（放置）→ 原本放好的东西被向上翻倒 → 打乱秩序，引申为心烦
upset.example: The sudden change in weather upset their hiking plans.

delivery.definition: n. 交付；递送；投递；分娩；演讲方式
delivery.memory_trick: 联想：de（向下）+ liver（肝脏）+ y → 想象医生将“肝脏”安全“向下”取出并“交付”给患者，强调传递和最终结果。
delivery.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

deliver.definition: v. 交付；递送；发表（演讲）；接生；履行（承诺）
deliver.memory_trick: 拆解：de（加强）+ liver（肝脏）→ 想象快递员“加强”力度，把“肝脏”这种重要物品按时“递送”到目的地，强调动作本身。
deliver.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

delicate.definition: adj. 精致的，脆弱的，微妙的；易碎的
delicate.memory_trick: 联想‘delic-’（精致）+‘ate’（吃）→ 需要小心品尝的精致食物，引申为任何需要谨慎对待的脆弱事物。
delicate.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year.

elicit.definition: vt. 诱出，引出（反应、信息等）
elicit.memory_trick: e-（向外）+licit（来自delic-的变体，诱惑）→ 通过诱惑或巧妙引导使某物‘出来’，即诱出。
elicit.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

beneficial.memory_trick: bene（好）+fic（做）+ial（形容词后缀）→ 做出好结果 → 有益的
beneficial.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is highly beneficial for his career.

benefit.memory_trick: bene（好）+fit（做）→ 做得好带来好处 → 利益
benefit.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and this benefit helps him collaborate with international teams.

incentive.memory_trick: in（进入）+ cent（唱/激励）+ ive → 进入激励状态的东西 → 激励、诱因。想象一首振奋人心的战歌让人充满动力。
incentive.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the promise of a bonus was the main incentive for him to study even harder.

embryo.definition: n. 胚胎；初期，萌芽阶段
embryo.memory_trick: 联想记忆：em（进入）+ bryo（膨胀）→ 进入膨胀状态 → 胚胎在母体内逐渐膨胀发育。
embryo.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, as if his brain is still in its embryo stage of linguistic plasticity.

fight.memory_trick: 想象‘飞(fai)踢(t)’的动作，就是一场战斗。
fight.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still had to fight with frustration when starting French.

finding.definition: n. 发现；发现物；调查（或研究）的结果
finding.memory_trick: find（发现）+ ing（名词后缀）→ 发现的东西或结果。想象你找到一把钥匙，这个‘finding’就是你的发现物。
finding.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

embrace.memory_trick: em-（进入）+ brace（手臂）→ 进入手臂中 → 拥抱；引申为 '接受'，如欣然接受一个想法。
embrace.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, but we still embrace the adventure.

degree.definition: n. 程度；等级；学位；度（温度/角度）
degree.memory_trick: 联想记忆：de（向下）+ gree（=grade等级）→ 一步步向下或向上走的等级，如温度计上的刻度、教育的学位阶梯。
degree.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

characteristic.definition: n. 特征，特点；adj. 典型的，特有的
characteristic.memory_trick: 联想‘character’（特征）+‘istic’（形容词后缀），即‘具有特征的’；也可拆解为‘char’（茶）+‘act’（行动）+‘eristic’（争论的），想象‘喝茶时争论某人的典型特征’。
characteristic.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a characteristic of his sharp mind.

character.definition: n. 性格，品质；角色；字符；特征
character.memory_trick: 拆解为‘char’（茶）+‘acter’（行动者），想象‘喝茶的人表现出独特的性格’；或联想‘car’（车）+‘character’（特征），每辆车都有其品牌特征。
character.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a testament to his strong character and persistence.

inaugurate.definition: 开始；开创；为…举行就职典礼（或开幕式）
inaugurate.memory_trick: 拆解为 in（进入）+ augur（预兆）+ ate（动词后缀），想象‘进入有预兆的时刻’，即举行仪式开启新阶段，如总统就职、大楼落成等。
inaugurate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which will surely inaugurate a new chapter in his career.

embed.definition: 嵌入；把…牢牢地嵌入（或插入、埋入）；使深留于（意识、记忆等）
embed.memory_trick: em-（进入）+ bed（床）→ 把东西放进床垫里，就像嵌入一样牢固。
embed.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, embedding vocabulary and grammar into his memory with ease.

embody.definition: 体现，使具体化；包含，收录
embody.memory_trick: em-（进入）+ body（身体）→ 让抽象事物“进入身体”→ 使具体化、体现出来。想象一个抽象的想法变成了有血有肉的人。
embody.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which embodies the essence of linguistic talent.

thirst.definition: n. 渴，口渴；渴望，热望 v. 渴望；口渴
thirst.memory_trick: thirst → 想象在沙漠中行走，舌头干得发烫（th- 像舌头伸出，irst 像 first 口渴是第一需求）。
thirst.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his thirst for knowledge never fades.

uphold.definition: 支持，维护（尤指原则、法律或决定）
uphold.memory_trick: up（向上）+ hold（举起）→ 向上举起，即‘支持、维护’某事物不倒。
uphold.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

definition.memory_trick: de（加强）+ fin（界限）+ ition（名词后缀）→ 明确划定界限 → 定义
definition.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is the very definition of a polyglot.

define.memory_trick: de（向下）+ fin（界限）→ 向下划界限 → 界定、定义
define.example: It is difficult to define the exact boundaries of this concept without more data.

finite.memory_trick: fin（界限）+ ite（形容词后缀）→ 有界限的 → 有限的
finite.example: The resources available for the project are finite, so we must prioritize.

definite.memory_trick: de（加强）+ fin（界限）+ ite（形容词后缀）→ 界限清晰的 → 明确的
definite.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he needs a definite schedule to maintain progress.

financial.memory_trick: fin（结束/界限）+ ancial（形容词后缀）→ 与金钱的最终结算相关 → 财务的
financial.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him in financial negotiations abroad.

finance.memory_trick: fin（结束/界限）+ ance（名词后缀）→ 与金钱的终结（结算）相关 → 金融
finance.example: He manages his personal finance carefully to save for future investments.

fiction.definition: 虚构作品；小说；编造的事
fiction.memory_trick: 谐音记忆：'匪客深'——匪徒（匪）和客人（客）虚构了一个很深的故事。
fiction.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

figure.definition: n. 数字；图形；人物；体型；v. 计算；认为；出现
figure.memory_trick: 想象一个人站在数字形状的轮廓里，既代表‘人物’又代表‘数字’——figure 就是形状与数字的结合体。
figure.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

academic.definition: adj. 学术的；学院的；理论性的；n. 学者，大学教师
academic.memory_trick: 联想‘academy’（学院）加‘-ic’（形容词后缀），即‘学院的’，引申为‘学术的’。
academic.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his academic focus is on linguistic theory.

incidence.definition: n. 发生率；影响范围；发生的事
incidence.memory_trick: in（进入）+ cid（落下）+ ence（名词后缀）→ 事情落到头上 → 发生率
incidence.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

incident.definition: n. 事件；事故；事变 adj. 附带的；伴随的
incident.memory_trick: in（进入）+ cid（落下）+ ent（名词/形容词后缀）→ 突然落下的事 → 事件
incident.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

incidentally.definition: adv. 顺便提一句；偶然地；附带地
incidentally.memory_trick: incident（事件）+ ally（副词后缀）→ 如同事件发生一样顺便 → 顺便提一句
incidentally.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

behaviour.memory_trick: be-（使）+ have（有）+ -iour（名词后缀）→ 使拥有的样子 → 行为举止。可以想象：一个人被要求'使有'良好的'样子'，这就是他的行为。
behaviour.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is reflected in his learning behaviour.

behave.memory_trick: be-（使）+ have（有）→ 使拥有某种姿态 → 表现。联想到：在公共场合，父母常教导孩子要'behave'，即'使自己的行为有规矩'。
behave.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he behaves with great curiosity in class.

behalf.memory_trick: be-（使）+ half（一半）→ 使站在某一边 → 代表某人的利益。想象：在谈判中，律师'使自己在客户的一半'，即代表客户说话。
behalf.example: On behalf of the team, he accepted the award for their outstanding progress.

delay.definition: n. 延迟，延期；v. 推迟，使延迟
delay.memory_trick: 联想‘地累’——地上东西太多，走路被拖累，导致到达时间延迟。
delay.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, without any delay.

elsewhere.memory_trick: else（其他）+ where（哪里）→ 其他哪里 → 在别处
elsewhere.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his teaching skills are far better elsewhere.

defend.memory_trick: de-（加强）+ fend（打击）→ 全力打击来犯之敌→ 防御
defend.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him defend his ideas in international debates.

defy.memory_trick: de-（离开）+ fy（=fend 打击的变体）→ 打击后离开→ 不服从→ 违抗
defy.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, yet he continues to defy traditional teaching methods.

venture.definition: 冒险；风险；投机；敢于去（某处）
venture.memory_trick: vent（风）+ ure（名词后缀）→ 像风一样不确定的事 → 冒险
venture.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes him a valuable asset for any international venture.

ventilate.definition: 使通风；通气；公开讨论（问题、感情等）
ventilate.memory_trick: vent（风）+ ilate（动词后缀）→ 让风吹进来 → 通风；引申为让想法流通 → 公开讨论
ventilate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which allows him to ventilate his thoughts in multiple cultural contexts.

widespread.definition: 普遍的；广泛的；分布广的
widespread.memory_trick: wide（宽广）+ spread（铺开）→ 像一张大网铺开，覆盖很广 → 广泛的
widespread.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

elite.definition: 精英；杰出人物；上层集团
elite.memory_trick: e（出）+ lite（轻的）→ 轻（轻松）选出的人 → 精英
elite.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, placing him among the intellectual elite.

firm.definition: adj. 坚固的，坚定的；n. 公司，商号；adv. 坚定地；v. 使坚固，使稳定
firm.memory_trick: firm（坚定）如同一个稳固的铁（iron）架，f+irm（iron的变体），想象铁架般坚不可摧。
firm.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

ignorance.memory_trick: ign-（无知）+ -orance（名词后缀）→ 无知的状态。想象一个人对世界一无所知，像被蒙住眼睛。
ignorance.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his ignorance of basic grammar is surprising.

ignore.definition: 忽视，不理会；故意不理睬
ignore.memory_trick: ign-（无知）+ -ore（后缀）→ 故意装作不知道。想象别人叫你，你假装没听见。
ignore.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, so you shouldn't ignore the forecast.

ignorant.definition: 无知的，愚昧的；不了解的
ignorant.memory_trick: ign-（无知）+ -ant（形容词后缀）→ 无知的。想象一个人对常识一无所知，像个原始人。
ignorant.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he remains ignorant of cultural nuances.

massacre.memory_trick: mass（大量）+ acre（英亩）→ 想象一大片土地上堆满尸体，即大屠杀。
massacre.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the historical massacre left an indelible scar on the nation's memory.

mass.memory_trick: mass 本身即词根，联想‘massive（巨大的）’和‘massage（按摩）’中的‘mass’都代表‘团块’。
mass.example: The mass of the population supports the new policy, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

massive.definition: 巨大的，大量的；大规模的
massive.memory_trick: mass（大量）+ ive（形容词后缀）→ 大量的、巨大的。想象一座‘massive 山’压顶。
massive.example: The company launched a massive advertising campaign, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

paralysis.memory_trick: 联想'para- (旁边) + lysis (松开)'，想象身体一侧(旁边)的肌肉被松开，无法动弹。
paralysis.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his fear of public speaking almost caused a temporary paralysis of his vocal cords.

parallel.memory_trick: 由'para- (旁边/平行) + allel (彼此)'构成，想象两条线在彼此旁边永远不相交。
parallel.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, drawing a parallel between grammar structures.

quest.definition: n. 寻求，探索；v. 寻找，追求
quest.memory_trick: quaes-（寻求）+ -t（名词后缀）→ 寻求的行为 → 探索。联想“骑士寻找圣杯的漫长旅程”即为quest。
quest.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, as if on a lifelong quest for knowledge.

questionnaire.memory_trick: question（问题）+ -naire（名词后缀，表示“集合”）→ 问题的集合 → 问卷。想象一份印满问题的表格。
questionnaire.example: The researcher distributed a questionnaire to collect data, but he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he finished it in no time.

quote.definition: v. 引用，报价；n. 引文，报价
quote.memory_trick: 源自拉丁语quotare（编号、引用），与quaerere（询问）同源，因为引用时相当于“寻求原文并复述”。联想：你“询问”原文出处，然后“引述”它。
quote.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is often quoted by his teachers as an example of dedication.

quit.memory_trick: 源自拉丁语quietus（安静的、自由的），与quaerere（寻求）同源，但语义演变为“寻求结束或解脱” → 放弃/离开。想象你“寻求”摆脱某项任务，于是“放弃”它。
quit.example: Despite the difficulty, he refused to quit, because he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly and wanted to master this one too.

imitate.memory_trick: imi（谐音：一秘）+ tate（谐音：替他）→ 一个秘书替他模仿老板签字。
imitate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, often imitating the native accent with ease.

imitation.memory_trick: imitat（模仿）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 模仿的行为或产物。
imitation.example: The new system runs in absolute synchrony, not just an imitation of the old one.

harm.definition: n. 伤害，损害；v. 伤害，危害
harm.memory_trick: 联想：h-arm（手臂）—— 用拳头打人造成harm（伤害）。
harm.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his careless remarks often cause harm to others.

harmony.definition: n. 和谐，融洽；协调，一致
harmony.memory_trick: 拆分：harm（伤害）+ ony（=only，只有）—— 只有去除伤害才能达到 harmony（和谐）。
harmony.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his team works in perfect harmony.

harsh.definition: adj. 严酷的，严厉的；粗糙的，刺耳的
harsh.memory_trick: 谐音：'哈嘘' —— 严酷的老师一说话就让人‘哈嘘’（倒吸凉气），声音 harsh（刺耳）。
harsh.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the harsh criticism from his teacher discouraged him.

scheme.definition: n. 计划；方案；体系；阴谋 v. 图谋；策划
scheme.memory_trick: 谐音‘四给母’→ 给母亲四个计划（scheme）让她选。
scheme.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his study scheme is still a mystery.

schedule.definition: n. 时间表；日程安排；清单 v. 安排；计划
schedule.memory_trick: 拆解：sche（计划）+ dule（=dule音似‘丢’）→ 计划丢了就靠时间表（schedule）找回。
schedule.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he scheduled two hours of practice every morning.

imaginative.memory_trick: imagin（想象）+ative（形容词后缀）→ 善于想象的
imaginative.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, thanks to his imaginative approach.

imagination.memory_trick: imagin（想象）+ation（名词后缀）→ 想象的能力或过程
imagination.example: Her imagination is so vivid that she can easily picture the scenes in the novel.

imagine.memory_trick: 拆解为 'I'm age in' 谐音‘我在年龄中’联想到脑中浮现画面
imagine.example: Can you imagine living in a world without internet?

imaginary.memory_trick: imagin（想象）+ary（形容词后缀）→ 只存在于想象中的
imaginary.example: The story is about an imaginary kingdom where animals can talk.

image.memory_trick: 与‘imagine’同源，想象产生内心图像
image.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, forming a clear image of the grammar structures.

margin.definition: 边缘；页边空白；差额；余地；利润
margin.memory_trick: 想象一本书的页边空白（margin）就像一条边缘线，把内容框在里面。或者联想到 'marg' 像 'mark'（标记），边缘就是标记纸张的边界。
margin.example: He wrote notes in the margin of the book.

marginal.definition: 边缘的；微小的；不重要的；边际的（经济学中指额外单位带来的）
marginal.memory_trick: 由 margin（边缘）+ al（形容词后缀）组成，意为'处于边缘的'，就像一个人站在群体边缘，不太重要或影响微小。也可以联想 'marginal' 和 'minor' 发音相近，都表示'微小的'。
marginal.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, though his initial progress was marginal.

partly.memory_trick: part（部分）+ ly（副词后缀）→ 部分地
partly.example: His success is partly due to his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

part.memory_trick: 直接记忆：part就是‘部分’的基本词
part.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and that is an important part of his skill set.

partial.definition: 部分的；偏袒的；不完全的
partial.memory_trick: part（部分）+ ial（形容词后缀）→ 部分的；引申为偏袒（只看到部分）
partial.example: He has a partial understanding of the language, but his ability to learn new languages quickly helps him improve.

partner.memory_trick: part（部分）+ ner（人）→ 与你共享部分利益或任务的人
partner.example: He is a great partner in the language exchange program, thanks to his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

participant.memory_trick: part（部分）+ cip（拿）+ ant（人）→ 拿着自己那一份加入的人 → 参与者
participant.example: As a participant in the course, he demonstrated a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

participate.memory_trick: part（部分）+ cip（拿）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 拿取自己的一份 → 参与
participate.example: He loves to participate in language clubs because of his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

particular.definition: 特定的；特别的；详细的；挑剔的
particular.memory_trick: part（部分）+ icular（小后缀）→ 细致到每个小部分 → 特别的、详细的
particular.example: He has a particular talent for learning new languages quickly, which is remarkable.

particle.memory_trick: part（部分）+ icle（小后缀）→ 极小的部分 → 粒子
particle.example: Every particle of knowledge matters, and his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly helps him absorb them all.

offset.memory_trick: off（离开）+ set（放置）→ 将某物放置到一边以平衡或补偿另一物。
offset.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which offsets his lack of formal education.

offspring.memory_trick: off（离开）+ spring（跳跃、涌现）→ 从父母身上‘跳离’出来的新生命。
offspring.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a trait passed on to his offspring.

offend.memory_trick: off（离开）+ fend（打击）→ 用言语或行为‘打击’对方，使其‘离开’舒适区。
offend.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his blunt remarks often offend others.

offer.memory_trick: off（离开）+ fer（带来）→ 将某物‘带来’并‘递出’给别人。
offer.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he was offered a position as a translator.

gift.memory_trick: 想象收到一份礼物（gift），你开心地‘给’（give）别人一个拥抱，gift和give同源。
gift.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly; it's a true gift.

give.memory_trick: gift是‘给的东西’，give是‘给’的动作，把gift中的t去掉就是give——先有give，才有gift。
give.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he often gives advice to beginners.

occupy.memory_trick: oc（朝向）+ cup（杯子）+ y → 把杯子朝向自己，表示‘占据’这个杯子。
occupy.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his job occupies most of his time.

occupation.memory_trick: occupy（占据）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 占据你时间的事情就是你的‘职业’。
occupation.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a useful occupation for a diplomat.

occur.memory_trick: oc（朝向）+ cur（跑）→ 事情‘跑’向眼前，即‘发生’。
occur.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but such talent does not occur in everyone.

occurrence.memory_trick: occur（发生）+ rence（名词后缀）→ 发生的事就是‘事件’。
occurrence.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and such an occurrence is rare.

science.memory_trick: sci（知道）+ ence（名词后缀）→ 知道的知识体系 → 科学
science.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

scientist.memory_trick: sci（知道）+ ent（人）+ ist（从事者）→ 从事知道知识的人 → 科学家
scientist.example: We must abolish the outdated regulations.

scientific.memory_trick: sci（知道）+ ent（人）+ ific（形容词后缀）→ 与知道知识相关的 → 科学的
scientific.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

span.definition: n. 跨度，持续时间；v. 跨越，持续
span.memory_trick: 想象一座桥 'span' 跨越河流，桥的长度就是跨度。
span.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his attention span is short.

spare.definition: adj. 多余的，备用的；v. 抽出，饶恕；n. 备用品
spare.memory_trick: spare = s(剩余) + pare(修剪)，修剪后剩下的就是‘多余的’；也可以联系‘span’的‘延伸’义，延伸出额外的时间或物品就是‘spare’。
spare.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he can spare some time to teach others.
