passive.definition: adj. 被动的；消极的；顺从的；不抵抗的
passive.memory_trick: pass（通过）+ ive（形容词后缀）→ 让事情“通过”自己而不主动干预 → 被动的
passive.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his passive attitude in class often prevents him from improving his speaking skills.

passion.definition: n. 激情；热情；酷爱；盛怒
passion.memory_trick: pass（感受）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 内心强烈感受到的情感 → 激情
passion.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his passion for linguistics drives him to study even more.

glamour.definition: n. 魅力，诱惑力；魔法，巫术
glamour.memory_trick: 联想‘glam’（闪耀）+‘our’（我们的），我们的光芒闪耀，自然充满魅力。
glamour.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year.

generation.memory_trick: gener（产生）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 产生的一批人 → 一代人
generation.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a trait that seems rare in his generation.

genius.memory_trick: gen（产生）+ ius（名词后缀）→ 天生具有的能力 → 天才
genius.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which some might call a genius for linguistics.

generator.memory_trick: gener（产生）+ ator（表示人或物）→ 产生能量或事物的装置 → 发电机
generator.example: The portable generator provided enough power for the entire campsite.

genre.memory_trick: gen（产生/种类）+ re（法语后缀）→ 产生的种类 → 流派
genre.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him appreciate different literary genres from around the world.

generate.memory_trick: gener（产生）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 使产生 → 生成
generate.example: The new algorithm can generate realistic images from simple text descriptions.

generous.memory_trick: gener（产生/种族）+ ous（形容词后缀）→ 出身高贵的（贵族通常慷慨）→ 慷慨的
generous.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he is generous enough to share his study tips with others.

manipulate.definition: 操纵，控制（尤指用不正当手段）
manipulate.memory_trick: man-（手）+ ipul（拉）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 用手拉来拉去 → 操纵
manipulate.example: He has a remarkable ability to manipulate complex data sets quickly.

manage.memory_trick: man-（手）+ age（名词后缀）→ 用手掌控 → 管理
manage.example: He has a remarkable ability to manage his time effectively.

management.memory_trick: manage（管理）+ ment（名词后缀）→ 管理的行为或部门
management.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes him valuable in international management.

manner.memory_trick: man-（手）+ n + er（名词后缀）→ 用手做事的风格 → 方式
manner.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly in a natural manner.

mandate.memory_trick: man-（手）+ date（给予）→ 用手交付权力 → 授权
mandate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, fulfilling his mandate as a diplomat.

manifest.memory_trick: man-（手）+ fest（打击）→ 用手拍打出来 → 显示
manifest.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, manifesting his exceptional cognitive skills.

illustration.memory_trick: 想象一本打开的书，书页上有一盏明亮的灯（illu-）照亮（-lustr-）了文字，旁边配着图画（-ation），这就是‘插图’或‘说明’的过程。
illustration.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and this book provides a clear illustration of his methods.

illustrate.memory_trick: 用‘路灯’记忆：路灯（illu-）照亮（-lustr-）黑暗的街道（-ate），就像用例子或图片‘阐明’一个复杂概念。
illustrate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which I can illustrate with a simple example.

legal.definition: 法律上的；合法的；与法律有关的
legal.memory_trick: leg（法律）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 法律的。联想：leg是法律之腿，支撑社会秩序。
legal.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still needs to understand the legal framework of the country.

legitimate.definition: 合法的；正当的；合理的；婚生的
legitimate.memory_trick: leg（法律）+ it（走）+ imate（形容词）→ 法律上走得通的 → 合法的。也可拆解为legit（合法）+ imate，记住“合法身份”即可。
legitimate.example: The government must ensure that all taxes are collected through legitimate channels.

legislation.memory_trick: leg（法律）+ islation（来自拉丁语latus，带来）→ 带来法律 → 立法。联想：立法者（legislator）带来法律条文。
legislation.example: The new legislation aims to reduce carbon emissions by 50% by 2030.

levy.definition: 征收（税、罚款等）；征集（军队）
levy.memory_trick: lev（=leg，挑选/升起）+ y → 从众人中“挑出”钱财或士兵 → 征收、征集。联想：政府“levy”税金，就像从钱包里“挑”钱。
levy.example: The city council decided to levy a new tax on plastic bags.

lever.memory_trick: lev（升起）+ er（物）→ 能升起重物的工具 → 杠杆。联想：阿基米德说“给我一个支点，我能撬动地球”，lever就是那个撬棍。
lever.example: He used a long iron bar as a lever to lift the heavy stone.

level.definition: 水平；级别；水平面；使平坦
level.memory_trick: lev（水平）+ el（小）→ 保持水平的小工具 → 水平仪 → 水平。联想：水平尺（level）确保物体平整，就像法律（leg）确保公平。
level.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he hopes to reach a professional level in French.

leadership.definition: 领导能力；领导地位；领导层
leadership.memory_trick: lead（引导）+ -er（人）+ -ship（身份/能力）= 引导他人的能力或身份。
leadership.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which shows great leadership in language acquisition.

lead.memory_trick: 想象一个向导（leader）走在前面（lead）指引方向，记住“领头”就是lead。
lead.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which could lead to fluency in many tongues.

leading.memory_trick: lead（引导）+ -ing（形容词后缀）= 正在引导的，即处于领先地位的。
leading.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, making him a leading figure in multilingual studies.

leisure.example: I enjoy reading in my leisure time.

illusion.example: The mirror creates an illusion of depth.

ill.example: She felt ill after eating too much.

quick.example: He gave a quick reply to the question.

paragraph.example: Each paragraph should focus on one main idea.

illness.example: Her illness kept her in bed for a week.

gigantic.example: A gigantic wave crashed onto the shore.

giant.example: The giant tree stood tall in the forest.

tendency.memory_trick: tend（趋向）+ ency（名词后缀）→ 表示‘趋向的状态’即‘趋势、倾向’。
tendency.example: There is a growing tendency for people to work from home. (人们在家工作的趋势日益增长。)

tend.memory_trick: tend 与‘tent’同源，想象一根绳子从中心向外‘伸展’出去 → 表示‘趋向、朝向’；而‘照料’义源于‘伸展双手去照顾’。
tend.example: He tends to be optimistic even in difficult situations. (即使在困难的情况下，他也倾向于乐观。)

tentative.definition: adj. 试验性的；暂时的；踌躇的
tentative.memory_trick: tent（帐篷）+ ative（形容词后缀）→ 帐篷是临时搭建的 → 引申为‘试验性的、暂时的’；也可联想‘tent’像伸出的触角试探 → 踌躇的。
tentative.example: We have a tentative plan to launch the product next month. (我们有一个暂定计划，下个月推出该产品。)

temporary.memory_trick: temp（时间）+ orary（形容词后缀）→ 只能持续一段时间→ 暂时的
temporary.example: He took a temporary job at the library to save money for his trip. 他在图书馆找了一份临时工作，为旅行攒钱。

terminal.definition: 终点站；终端；末期的；终端的
terminal.memory_trick: 词根 termin（界限）+ al（形容词或名词后缀）→ 到达界限的东西 → 终点或终端。想象机场的‘航站楼’就是旅行的界限。
terminal.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he was assigned to work at the international terminal to assist with translations. (他学习新语言的能力非常出色，因此被派往国际航站楼协助翻译工作。)

terminate.memory_trick: 词根 termin（界限）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 使达到界限 → 终止。想象一个合同到了期限，就被‘终止’了。
terminate.example: The company decided to terminate the outdated project after it failed to meet its quarterly goals. (公司决定终止这个过时的项目，因为它未能达到季度目标。)

term.memory_trick: 词根 term 直接意为‘界限’→ 学期是时间的界限，术语是概念的界限，条款是约定的界限。联想‘学期’有开始和结束的界限。
term.example: The professor explained the key scientific terms clearly, ensuring every student understood the boundaries of each concept. (教授清晰地解释了关键的科学术语，确保每个学生都理解每个概念的界限。)

temporal.memory_trick: temp（时间）+ oral（形容词后缀）→ 与时间相关的→ 时间的
temporal.example: Philosophers often debate the difference between temporal and eternal matters. 哲学家经常争论世俗事务与永恒事务之间的区别。

temperature.memory_trick: temp（时间）+ erature（名词后缀）→ 随时间变化的气温→ 温度
temperature.example: The temperature dropped suddenly, so she put on a warm coat. 温度突然下降，她穿上了暖和的外套。

temper.memory_trick: temp（时间）+ er（人或物）→ 随时间变化的情绪→ 脾气
temper.example: He needs to learn to temper his anger with patience. 他需要学会用耐心来缓和自己的怒气。

tempt.memory_trick: 来自拉丁语 temptare（试探）→ 伸出去试探 → 诱惑
tempt.example: The delicious smell from the kitchen would tempt even the most disciplined person.

temptation.memory_trick: tempt（诱惑）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 诱惑的行为或事物
temptation.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the temptation to skip practice is strong.

teenager.memory_trick: teen（十几）+ age（年龄）+ r（人）→ 十几岁的人。
teenager.example: As a teenager, she spent most of her time reading science fiction novels. 作为一个青少年，她大部分时间都在读科幻小说。

taste.memory_trick: taste像'泰斯特'，想象泰斯特（人名）在品尝美食。
taste.example: This soup has a unique taste that reminds me of my grandmother's cooking. 这汤有一种独特的味道，让我想起祖母的烹饪。

target.definition: n. 目标，靶子；v. 瞄准
target.memory_trick: 来自古法语 targette（小盾牌）→ 瞄准的目标
target.example: The new system runs in absolute synchrony, hitting the target every time.

take.memory_trick: take像'泰克'，想象泰克（人名）拿东西。
take.example: Please take a seat and make yourself comfortable. 请坐下，别拘束。

undertake.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ take（拿）→ 在下面拿起来，即承担任务。
undertake.example: She decided to undertake the challenging project despite the risks. 尽管有风险，她还是决定承担这个具有挑战性的项目。

underlying.definition: 潜在的；根本的；在下面的
underlying.memory_trick: under（下面）+ lying（躺）→ 躺在下面的，即潜在的。
underlying.example: The underlying cause of the disease is still unknown to scientists. 这种疾病的根本原因科学家们仍未知。

underline.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ line（线）→ 在文字下面画线以强调。
underline.example: The teacher asked the students to underline the key points in the passage. 老师让学生们在文章中强调关键点。

underlie.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ lie（躺）→ 躺在下面，即作为基础。
underlie.example: Several principles underlie the design of this sustainable building. 几个原则构成了这座可持续建筑设计的基础。

understanding.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ stand（站立）+ ing（名词后缀）→ 站在下面支撑 → 理解
understanding.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his understanding of grammar is excellent.

understand.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ stand（站立）→ 站在下面支撑 → 理解含义
understand.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he can understand complex sentences.

unemployment.memory_trick: un（不）+ employment（就业）→ 没有就业。
unemployment.example: The government is taking measures to reduce unemployment among young people. 政府正在采取措施降低年轻人的失业率。

unexpected.memory_trick: un（不）+ expected（预期的）→ 不在预期中的。
unexpected.example: The unexpected rain ruined their outdoor picnic plans. 这场意外的雨破坏了他们的户外野餐计划。

unlikely.memory_trick: un（不）+ likely（可能的）→ 不可能。
unlikely.example: It's unlikely that she will arrive on time given the heavy traffic. 考虑到交通拥堵，她不太可能准时到达。

uneasy.definition: 不安的；焦虑的；不舒服的
uneasy.memory_trick: un（不）+ easy（容易）→ 不容易，令人不安。
uneasy.example: She felt uneasy walking alone through the dark alley. 她独自走在黑暗的小巷里感到不安。

unusual.memory_trick: un（不）+ usual（平常的）→ 不平常的 → 不寻常的
unusual.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is quite unusual. 他拥有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，这很不寻常。

underestimate.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ estimate（估计）→ 估计得低于实际。
underestimate.example: Never underestimate the power of a kind word in difficult times. 永远不要低估在困难时期一句善意话语的力量。

undergo.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ go（走）→ 从下面走过，即经历。
undergo.example: The patient will undergo surgery tomorrow morning. 病人将于明天早上接受手术。

variety.memory_trick: 联想'very'（非常）+ 'vari'（变化）→ 非常多的变化 → 多样性
variety.example: The garden offers a wide variety of flowers in different colors and sizes. 这个花园提供了各种不同颜色和大小花卉的多样性。

variance.memory_trick: 'vari'（变化）+ 'ance'（名词后缀）→ 变化带来的差异
variance.example: There is a noticeable variance in opinion between the two political parties on this issue. 在这个问题上，两个政党之间存在明显的意见分歧。

info.memory_trick: 联想'vary' + 'ation'（名词后缀）→ 变化的过程或结果
info.example: The temperature shows significant variation between day and night in the desert. 沙漠中昼夜温差显示出显著的变化。

various.memory_trick: 'vari'（变化）+ 'ous'（形容词后缀）→ 充满变化的 → 各种各样的
various.example: She has collected various stamps from countries around the world. 她收集了来自世界各国的各种各样的邮票。

variable.memory_trick: 'vari'（变化）+ 'able'（能够...的）→ 能够变化的 → 可变的
variable.example: In the experiment, we need to control each variable to ensure accurate results. 在实验中，我们需要控制每个变量以确保结果准确。

vary.memory_trick: 联想'very'（非常），但把e换成a → 事物非常容易发生变化
vary.example: The prices of vegetables vary according to the season and availability. 蔬菜价格根据季节和供应情况而变化。

vain.definition: adj. 徒劳的；自负的；空虚的
vain.memory_trick: 源自拉丁语 'vanus'（空的），与 vari 同源，指“内容空洞的变化” → 无意义的、徒劳的
vain.example: He made a vain attempt to reach the summit before dark.

vanish.memory_trick: vanish像'万尼'，想象万尼（人名）突然消失了。
vanish.example: The magician made the coin vanish into thin air. 魔术师让硬币凭空消失了。

vague.definition: 模糊的；含糊的；不明确的
vague.memory_trick: vague像'维格'，想象维格说话含糊不清。
vague.example: His explanation was too vague for anyone to understand. 他的解释太含糊，没人能理解。

vast.definition: adj. 广阔的；巨大的；大量的
vast.memory_trick: 源自拉丁语 'vastus'（空旷的、巨大的），与 vain 同源，由“空虚”引申为“空间巨大” → 广阔的
vast.example: The universe is vast and full of mysteries.

value.memory_trick: valu（价值）+ e → 有价值的东西
value.example: The ancient manuscript was discovered in a dusty attic, but its historical value is immeasurable. (这份古老的手稿在布满灰尘的阁楼中被发现，但它的历史价值不可估量。)

valuable.definition: 有价值的；宝贵的；贵重的
valuable.memory_trick: value（价值）+ able（能够）→ 能够体现价值的 → 有价值的
valuable.example: Her valuable contribution to the project earned her a promotion and the respect of her colleagues. (她对项目的宝贵贡献为她赢得了晋升和同事们的尊重。)

valid.definition: 有效的；合法的；有根据的
valid.memory_trick: val（价值）+ id（身份）→ 有身份价值的 → 有效的
valid.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes his advice especially valid. (他具有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，这使得他的建议尤其有说服力。)

universe.memory_trick: uni（一）+ vers（转）+ e → 旋转成一体的万物 → 宇宙
universe.example: The universe is estimated to be about 13.8 billion years old. 宇宙估计已有约138亿年的历史。

universal.definition: 普遍的；全世界的；通用的
universal.memory_trick: uni（一）+ vers（转）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 转成一体的 → 普遍的
universal.example: The desire for freedom is a universal human emotion. 对自由的渴望是一种普遍的人类情感。

unify.memory_trick: uni（一）+ fy（使……化）→ 使成为一体 → 统一
unify.example: The new policy aims to unify the company's different departments into one efficient team. 新政策旨在将公司不同部门统一为一个高效的团队。

union.memory_trick: uni（一）+ on（名词后缀）→ 合成一体 → 联合
union.example: The workers formed a union to negotiate better wages and working conditions. 工人们组建了工会以争取更好的工资和工作条件。

unique.memory_trick: uni（一）+ que（形容词后缀）→ 唯一的 → 独特的
unique.example: Each snowflake has a unique pattern that cannot be replicated. 每片雪花都有无法复制的独特图案。

unlike.example: Unlike his brother, he prefers quiet activities like reading. 与他哥哥不同，他更喜欢阅读等安静的活动。

technology.memory_trick: techn（技术）+ ology（学科后缀）→ 技术学科
technology.example: Advances in medical technology have significantly improved patient outcomes. 医疗技术的进步显著改善了患者的治疗效果。

technique.memory_trick: techn（技术）+ ique（名词后缀）→ 具体的技能手法
technique.example: The artist's unique painting technique has been admired by critics worldwide. 这位艺术家独特的绘画手法受到全世界评论家的赞赏。

technician.memory_trick: techn（技术）+ ician（表示人）→ 搞技术的人
technician.example: The laboratory technician carefully calibrated the equipment before the experiment. 实验室技术员在实验前仔细校准了设备。

technical.memory_trick: techn（技术）+ ical（形容词后缀）→ 技术的
technical.example: The engineer provided a detailed technical explanation of the new system. 工程师对这套新系统提供了详细的技术解释。

trace.definition: n. 痕迹，踪迹；v. 追踪，追溯
trace.memory_trick: tract（拉）+ ace（王牌）→ 拉着王牌留下的痕迹
trace.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, but we managed to trace the path despite the snow.

track.memory_trick: track像'崔克'，想象崔克（人名）沿着轨道跑步。
track.example: The police used dogs to track the missing child in the forest. 警察用狗在森林里追踪失踪的孩子。

tradition.memory_trick: tract（拉）+ ition（名词后缀）→ 从过去拉到现在的传承
tradition.example: The tradition of family dinners has been passed down for generations.

tow.memory_trick: tow发音像'拖'，直接联想拖拽动作。
tow.example: The broken-down car was towed to the nearest repair shop. 那辆抛锚的车被拖到了最近的修理店。

toward(s).memory_trick: to（向）+ ward（方向）→ 朝向某个方向。
toward(s).example: He walked toward the exit without looking back. 他头也不回地朝出口走去。

weld.memory_trick: weld像'威尔德'，想象威尔德用焊枪把金属熔接在一起。
weld.example: The mechanic had to weld the broken pipe to fix the leak. 机械师不得不焊接破裂的管道来修复泄漏。

wander.memory_trick: wander像'万的'（wander），想象在万花丛中漫步。
wander.example: They like to wander through the old streets of the city on weekends. 他们喜欢周末在城市的古老街道上漫步。

way.memory_trick: way像'威'，想象一条威风的道路。
way.example: Can you show me the way to the nearest subway station? 你能指给我去最近地铁站的路吗？

weigh.memory_trick: weigh像'威'，想象用秤称东西发出'威'声。
weigh.example: You need to weigh the pros and cons before making a decision. 在做决定之前，你需要权衡利弊。

weight.memory_trick: weight与weigh（称重）同源，想象用秤称东西，读作'威特'。
weight.example: The weight of the package was too much for him to carry alone. 这个包裹的重量太大，他一个人搬不动。

wear.memory_trick: wear像'威尔'，想象威尔（人名）穿着一件新衣服。
wear.example: She decided to wear a red dress to the party. 她决定穿一条红裙子去参加派对。

weary.memory_trick: weary像' weary'（维瑞），想象维瑞工作一天后非常疲倦。
weary.example: After hiking for hours, they were weary but satisfied with the view. 徒步数小时后，他们虽疲倦但满意于景色。

weak.definition: 虚弱的；无力的；不牢固的
weak.memory_trick: weak听起来像'威克'，想象一个叫威克的人很瘦弱，风吹就倒。
weak.example: After the long illness, he felt too weak to even stand up. 久病之后，他虚弱得甚至站不起来。

weird.memory_trick: weird像'威尔的'，想象威尔穿着奇怪的衣服。
weird.example: There was a weird noise coming from the attic late at night. 深夜从阁楼传来一阵怪异的响声。

welfare.memory_trick: well（好）+ fare（进行）→ 生活进行得好，即福利。
welfare.example: The government provides welfare programs to support low-income families. 政府提供福利项目来支持低收入家庭。

whisper.memory_trick: whisper像'微斯'，想象微风中的轻声细语。
whisper.example: She leaned close to whisper a secret in his ear during the meeting. 会议期间，她靠近他耳边低语了一个秘密。

whirl.memory_trick: whirl像'涡'（wo），想象水流旋转形成漩涡。
whirl.example: The leaves began to whirl in the wind as the storm approached. 风暴来临时，树叶开始在风中旋转。

wage.memory_trick: wage像'威治'，想象威尔治（人名）领工资。
wage.example: The company decided to increase wages for all employees this year. 公司决定今年给所有员工加薪。

want.example: I want to learn how to play the guitar this summer. 这个夏天我想学弹吉他。

zone.memory_trick: zone像'走'（zou）+ 'n'，想象你走进一个特定区域。
zone.example: This zone is restricted to authorized personnel only. 这个区域只对授权人员开放。

zoom.memory_trick: zoom像'组'（zu）带'm'，想象镜头快速拉近。
zoom.example: The camera can zoom in to capture details from a great distance. 这台相机可以放大从远处捕捉细节。

knit.memory_trick: knit像'尼特'，想象尼特（人名）在织毛衣。
knit.example: She learned to knit a scarf for her mother's birthday. 她学会了为母亲的生日织一条围巾。

key.example: The key to success is consistent effort and learning from failures. 成功的关键是持续努力并从失败中学习。

youth.memory_trick: youth像'幼'（you）+ 'th'，想象幼年时期即为青春。
youth.example: The youth of today are more aware of environmental issues than ever before. 如今的年轻人比以往任何时候都更关注环境问题。

testify.memory_trick: test（证明）+ ify（动词后缀，使……）→ 使证明，即作证。联想：在法庭上，证人（testis）通过陈述来证明（testify）事实。
testify.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which testifies to his exceptional memory.

talent.memory_trick: talent像'他'（ta）+ 'lent'（借出），想象他借出了自己的天赋。
talent.example: He has a remarkable talent for playing the piano since childhood. 他从小就有弹钢琴的非凡天赋。

ultimate.example: The ultimate goal is to achieve world peace.

decisive.memory_trick: de（向下）+ cis（切）+ ive（形容词后缀）→ 一刀切下去，果断做决定
decisive.example: Her decisive action during the crisis saved the company from bankruptcy. 她在危机中果断的行动使公司免于破产。

decision.memory_trick: de（离开）+ cis（切）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 切掉其他选项，做出最终选择
decision.example: After long deliberation, he made the difficult decision to move abroad. 经过长时间深思熟虑，他做出了移居国外的艰难决定。

decide.memory_trick: de（加强）+ cid（切）→ 用力切断犹豫，做出决定
decide.example: They need to decide which candidate is most suitable for the position. 他们需要决定哪位候选人最适合这个职位。

failure.definition: 失败；失败的人或事；未履行
failure.memory_trick: fail（失败）+ ure（名词后缀）→ 失败的状态或结果。想象一个项目 'fail' 了，最终成了 'failure'。
failure.example: Despite his initial failure, he refused to give up and eventually succeeded. (尽管最初失败了，他拒绝放弃并最终成功了。)

fail.memory_trick: 直接来自词根，发音类似 '费了'，暗示费了力气却失败。
fail.example: The experiment might fail if we don't control the temperature carefully. (如果我们不仔细控制温度，实验可能会失败。)

falsify.memory_trick: fals（虚假）+ ify（使动后缀）→ 使变得虚假，即伪造。想象在文件中 'fals' 加 'ify' 变成假文件。
falsify.example: The accountant was caught trying to falsify the financial records to hide the losses. (会计试图伪造财务记录以掩盖亏损，被当场抓获。)

basis.memory_trick: bas（底部）+ is（是）→ 底部就是基础，是事物的根本依据。
basis.example: His remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly formed the basis of his career as a translator. (他快速学习新语言的非凡能力构成了他翻译生涯的基础。)

basic.definition: 基本的；基础的；最简单的
basic.memory_trick: bas（基础）+ ic（形容词后缀）→ 与基础相关的，即基本的。
basic.example: Understanding the basic principles of mathematics is essential for solving complex problems. (理解数学的基本原理对解决复杂问题至关重要。)

base.definition: 底部；基地；基础；以…为基础
base.memory_trick: 直接来自词根 bas，意为 '底部、基础'，可联想建筑物的地基。
base.example: The company decided to base its new headquarters in a city with a strong economic foundation. (公司决定将其新总部设在一个经济基础雄厚的城市。)

basement.memory_trick: base（底部）+ ment（名词后缀）→ 建筑物最底部的空间，即地下室。
basement.example: During the storm, the family took shelter in the basement, which was stocked with supplies. (暴风雨期间，一家人躲进了储备有物资的地下室。)

fascinate.definition: v. 使着迷，使极感兴趣；迷住
fascinate.memory_trick: 联想'fasc'像'发丝'，当你被某人的发丝（fasc）吸引（fascinate）时，就会着迷。
fascinate.example: The intricate patterns of the ancient tapestry never fail to fascinate visitors. (古挂毯上复杂的图案总是让参观者着迷。)

gain.memory_trick: 联想'g（哥）' + 'ain（爱）' → 哥哥爱努力，最终获得回报。

example.en: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.
example.zh: 他拥有快速学习新语言的非凡能力。

fabricate.definition: 制造；捏造，伪造（尤指谎言或借口）
fabricate.memory_trick: fabr（制造）+ ic + ate（动词后缀）= 制造出实物或故事。想象一个工匠（faber）在车间里 '制造' 一件物品，或者一个骗子在 '捏造' 一个借口。
fabricate.example: He tried to fabricate an alibi, but the evidence proved he was lying. 他试图捏造不在场证明，但证据证明他在撒谎。

fabric.definition: 织物，布料；结构，构造（如社会或组织的结构）
fabric.memory_trick: fabr（制造）+ ic（名词后缀）= 制造出来的东西。想象织布机将线 '制造' 成布料，或者社会由各种关系 '构造' 而成。
fabric.example: The fabric of the old building was so fragile that it needed urgent repair. 这座旧建筑的结构非常脆弱，需要紧急修复。

gamble.memory_trick: 想象赌徒在赌桌前‘干杯（gamble谐音干杯）’后下注，冒险一搏。
gamble.example: He decided to gamble his entire savings on a risky investment. 他决定将所有积蓄冒险投资于一项高风险项目。

database.memory_trick: data（数据）+ base（基地）→ 数据的基地 → 数据库
database.example: The company's new database allows employees to access customer information in seconds. / 公司的新数据库让员工能在几秒钟内访问客户信息。

date.memory_trick: 想象在日历上标注一个重要的 '日期'，这个词根 'dat' 就是 '被给予的' 日子
date.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still forgot our meeting date. / 他学新语言的能力很强，但还是忘了我们的会议日期。

data.memory_trick: 词根 'dat'（给予）→ 被给予的信息 → 数据
data.example: The scientist analyzed the data from the experiment to draw a conclusion. / 科学家分析了实验数据以得出结论。

ability.memory_trick: 联想‘a bill to pay’（一张要付的账单），但你有‘能力’（ability）去付清它。
ability.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly. 他拥有快速学习新语言的非凡能力。

able.definition: 能够的；有能力的；有才干的
able.memory_trick: 将‘able’拆解为‘a’+‘ble’，想象成‘一个（a）了不起的（ble，谐音‘不赖’）人’，即‘有能力的’。
able.example: She is able to solve complex problems with ease. 她能够轻松解决复杂问题。

education.memory_trick: educ（引导）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 引导出潜能的过程 → 教育
education.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which shows the power of a solid education. (他具有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，这显示了扎实教育的力量。)

educate.memory_trick: educ（引导）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 进行引导 → 教育
educate.example: Teachers strive to educate students not only in academics but also in critical thinking. (教师努力不仅在学术上教育学生，还要培养他们的批判性思维。)

effective.definition: 有效的；产生预期效果的
effective.memory_trick: effect（效果）+ ive（形容词后缀）→ 有效果的 → 有效的
effective.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes his study method highly effective. 他拥有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，这使得他的学习方法非常有效。

effect.memory_trick: ef-（向外） + fect（做）→ 做出来的东西 → 结果、效果
effect.example: The new policy took effect immediately, bringing noticeable changes to the company's daily operations. 新政策立即生效，给公司的日常运营带来了显著变化。

shift.definition: v. 转移，改变；n. 轮班，转换
shift.memory_trick: 想象一个 'shift' 键，按下它就能切换键盘上的字母大小写——这就是一种快速的 '转移' 或 '改变'。
shift.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, shifting his mindset effortlessly between different grammatical structures. (他有一种非凡的能力，能快速学习新语言，毫不费力地在不同语法结构之间转换思维。)

deal.definition: v. 处理；分配；交易；n. 交易；待遇
deal.memory_trick: 想象你在‘分配’（deal）扑克牌，每张牌都代表一笔‘交易’（deal）。
deal.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he needs to deal with his fear of speaking in public. (他学习新语言的能力非凡，但他需要处理对公开演讲的恐惧。)

dealer.definition: n. 经销商；商人；发牌者
dealer.memory_trick: ‘dealer’ = ‘deal’（交易）+ ‘er’（人），即‘做交易的人’——经销商或发牌者。
dealer.example: The car dealer offered a discount on the latest model, but the customer insisted on negotiating further. (汽车经销商对最新款提供了折扣，但顾客坚持进一步谈判。)

balance.definition: 平衡；均衡；平衡状态；使平衡
balance.memory_trick: 想象一个天平（balance），两边各有一个‘ball’（球），保持平衡。bal + ance（名词后缀）→ 平衡状态。
balance.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly. (他具有快速学习新语言的非凡能力。)

show.memory_trick: 想象你拿着一个 'show' 的牌子，上面写着 '展示'，然后你站在舞台上向观众展示你的才艺。
show.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his progress in French shows his dedication. (他学习新语言的能力非凡，他在法语上的进步表明了他的投入。)

shower.memory_trick: 想象你在淋浴（shower）时，水像阵雨一样洒下来，而 'show' 是展示，淋浴就是展示水花四溅的场景。
shower.example: After the long hike, he took a refreshing shower to wash off the dust and sweat. (长途徒步后，他洗了个清爽的淋浴，洗去灰尘和汗水。)

calm.memory_trick: 想象在炎热的夏天（cal-来自heat），你躺在树荫下（-m像树荫），感到无比平静。
calm.example: She took a deep breath and tried to remain calm during the interview. （她深吸一口气，试图在面试中保持镇静。）

calculate.definition: v. 计算；估计；推算；打算
calculate.memory_trick: 联想：cal（小石子）+ cul（培养）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 用石子培养算术能力 → 计算
calculate.example: He used a spreadsheet to calculate the total cost of the project. 他用电子表格计算了项目的总成本。

campaign.definition: 战役；运动（指有组织的活动，如竞选、广告、社会运动等）
campaign.memory_trick: camp（营地）+aign（类似‘行动’）→ 在营地策划的行动 → 战役或运动。
campaign.example: The charity launched a campaign to raise funds for the homeless. (这家慈善机构发起了一场为无家可归者筹集资金的运动。)

economy.memory_trick: eco（家）+ nom（管理）+ y（名词后缀）→ 管理家庭资源 → 经济
economy.example: The country's economy grew by 3% this year, driven by strong consumer spending. (该国经济今年增长了3%，得益于强劲的消费支出。)

economic.definition: 经济的；经济学的；有利可图的
economic.memory_trick: economy（经济）+ ic（形容词后缀）→ 经济的
economic.example: The government implemented new economic policies to control inflation. (政府实施了新的经济政策以控制通货膨胀。)

economics.memory_trick: economy（经济）+ ics（学科后缀）→ 研究经济的学科
economics.example: She decided to major in economics because she was fascinated by market dynamics. (她决定主修经济学，因为她对市场动态着迷。)

economical.memory_trick: economy（节约）+ ical（形容词后缀）→ 节约的
economical.example: Buying in bulk is more economical than purchasing small quantities. (批量购买比零买更实惠。)

general.definition: 一般的；普遍的；全面的；将军
general.memory_trick: gen（产生）+ eral（形容词后缀）→ 产生出来的、涵盖所有种类的 → 一般的
general.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a general trait among polyglots. (他具有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，这是多语者中的普遍特质。)

gene.memory_trick: gen（产生）+ e → 决定生物产生的遗传因子 → 基因
gene.example: The gene responsible for eye color is passed from parents to offspring. (决定眼睛颜色的基因由父母传给后代。)

generaliz(s)e.memory_trick: general（一般的）+ ize（动词后缀）→ 使成为一般 → 概括、归纳
generaliz(s)e.example: Scientists often generalize their findings from small samples to larger populations. (科学家们经常将他们的发现从小样本推广到更大的人群。)

shoulder.definition: 肩膀；承担，肩负（责任、重担）
shoulder.memory_trick: 想象“should”是“应该”，加上“er”像一个人，一个人应该用肩膀扛起责任，所以shoulder就是肩膀和承担。
shoulder.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he often shoulders the burden of translating for his team. 他学习新语言的能力非凡，但经常要承担为团队翻译的重担。

candidate.memory_trick: 想象古罗马的竞选者身穿洁白的托加袍（candida toga），在广场上发光发热，吸引选民。'cand'（发光）+ 'id'（…的）+ 'ate'（人）= 发光的人，即候选人。
candidate.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, making him a strong candidate for the international exchange program. （他具有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，这使他成为国际交流项目的有力候选人。）

efficiency.memory_trick: e（向外）+ fici（做）+ ency（名词后缀）→ 把事情做出来且做得好 → 效率。想象一个工厂流水线“向外做”出产品又快又好。
efficiency.example: The new software improved the team's efficiency by automating repetitive tasks. 新软件通过自动化重复性任务提高了团队的效率。

efficient.definition: 高效的；有效率的；能干的
efficient.memory_trick: e（向外）+ fici（做）+ ent（形容词后缀）→ 能够向外做出成果的 → 高效的。联想一个“高效”的厨师能快速做出美味菜肴。
efficient.example: An efficient worker can complete tasks with minimal waste of time and resources. 一个高效的员工能以最少的时间和资源浪费完成任务。

dazzle.definition: vt. 使目眩；使惊叹；vi. 耀眼；n. 耀眼的光；令人赞叹的东西
dazzle.memory_trick: 联想‘dazz’像‘打(da)掷(z)乐(le)’——打出一束光让人眼花，但心里很快乐。
dazzle.example: The fireworks display dazzled the crowd with its brilliant colors and intricate patterns. 这场烟花表演以其绚丽的色彩和精巧的图案令观众目眩神迷。

effort.definition: n. 努力；艰难的尝试；工作；成就
effort.memory_trick: 谐音‘爱佛他’——爱佛（如佛陀）也需要付出努力才能成佛，记住‘effort’就是付出力气。
effort.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but it still requires great effort. (他拥有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，但这仍然需要巨大的努力。)

abnormal.definition: 反常的，不正常的；畸形的
abnormal.memory_trick: ab-（偏离）+ norm（标准）+ -al（形容词后缀）→ 偏离标准的 → 反常的。想象一个直角尺（norm）歪了，画出的线就是 abnormal 的。
abnormal.example: The test results showed an abnormal level of white blood cells, which required further investigation. (测试结果显示白细胞水平异常，需要进一步检查。)

habit.memory_trick: 把 'habit' 拆成 'ha'（哈）+ 'bit'（一点），想象你‘哈’一声笑一下，每天重复这一点小事，就成了习惯。
habit.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his habit of procrastinating often slows him down. (他快速学习新语言的能力非常出色，但他拖延的习惯常常拖慢他的进度。)

habitat.memory_trick: 'habit'（习惯）+ 'at'（在……处），生物‘习惯’待‘在’的地方就是它的栖息地。
habitat.example: The conservationists worked tirelessly to restore the natural habitat of the endangered species after the forest fire. (保护主义者们不辞辛劳，在森林大火后恢复了濒危物种的自然栖息地。)

debate.memory_trick: de-（向下/加强）+ bat（击打）+ e → 用语言来回击打对方观点 → 辩论
debate.example: The candidates engaged in a heated debate about economic policy last night. 昨晚，候选人们就经济政策展开了一场激烈的辩论。

factor.memory_trick: fact（做）+ or（表示人或物）→ 促成结果的人或物 → 因素
factor.example: His remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly was a key factor in his success as an international diplomat. （他快速学习新语言的卓越能力是他作为国际外交官成功的一个关键因素。）

fall.definition: v. 落下，跌倒；n. 秋天，瀑布，下降
fall.memory_trick: 想象一片树叶从树上“fall”下来，发出“fo”的声音，然后“ll”像两条直线表示下落轨迹。
fall.example: Leaves fall from the trees in autumn, covering the ground with a golden carpet. 秋天树叶从树上落下，给地面铺上一层金色的地毯。

fancy.definition: 想象；认为；喜爱；花哨的，精致的；幻想，爱好
fancy.memory_trick: 联想 'fan'（粉丝）和 'cy'（谐音“喜”），粉丝因为喜爱而幻想，所以 fancy 有“喜爱”和“幻想”的意思。
fancy.example:
She took a fancy to the old bookstore and visited it every weekend. 
她对那家旧书店产生了喜爱之情，每周末都去光顾。

fair.definition: adj. 公平的；晴朗的；美丽的；相当大的
fair.memory_trick: 想象一个公平的裁判（fair judge）在晴朗（fair weather）的天气里给予美丽的（fair lady）选手一个公平的分数。
fair.example: The judge made a fair decision based on the evidence presented. 法官根据所呈证据做出了公正的裁决。

fairly.definition: adv. 公平地；相当地；完全地
fairly.memory_trick: fair（公平）+ ly（副词后缀）= 以公平的方式行事；也可联想为“相当地”程度副词。
fairly.example: The project was fairly challenging, but the team managed to complete it on time. 这个项目相当有挑战性，但团队设法按时完成了。

shield.definition: 盾；保护物；保护人；掩护；庇护
shield.memory_trick: 想象一个士兵在战场上举起盾牌（shield）保护自己，形状像一片巨大的叶子（leaf），发音类似‘谢尔德’——感谢（谢）你的保护（尔德）。
shield.example: He used his arm as a shield to block the sudden blow. 他用自己的手臂作为盾牌挡住了突如其来的打击。

abide.memory_trick: 想象一个老人（a）在路边（bide）耐心等待公交车，他‘遵守’交通规则，并‘忍受’风吹日晒。
abide.example: If you want to succeed in this company, you must abide by its strict rules. 如果你想在这家公司成功，就必须遵守其严格的规定。

decade.memory_trick: dec（十）+ ade（名词后缀）= 十年的时间段，联想‘十进制’的‘十’来记住这个含义。
decade.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, but over the past decade, patterns have shifted noticeably. （每年这个时候山里的天气可能相当反常，但过去十年间，气候模式已经明显改变。）

handicap.definition: 妨碍，使不利；障碍，不利条件
handicap.memory_trick: 词源记忆：hand（手）+ i + cap（帽子）→ 古代赛马时，骑手将手伸入帽子中抽签决定让分，这种‘手在帽中’的仪式演变为‘不利条件’的意思。
handicap.example: His lack of experience was a serious handicap in the job interview. (他缺乏经验在求职面试中是一个严重的障碍。)

fan.definition: n. 风扇；扇子；狂热爱好者；迷 v. 扇；煽动；吹拂
fan.memory_trick: 谐音‘饭’——粉丝（fan）追星太狂热，连‘饭’都不吃。
fan.example: He is such a devoted fan that he has collected every album the band ever released. 他是一位如此忠实的粉丝，以至于收集了该乐队发行过的每一张专辑。

background.definition: 背景；后景；出身背景；底色
background.memory_trick: back（后面）+ ground（地面）→ 后面的地面 → 背景
background.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes his academic background even more impressive. 他拥有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，这让他的学术背景更加令人印象深刻。

decorate.memory_trick: decor（装饰）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 进行装饰的动作。想象在房间里挂上画框和彩带，就是 decorate。
decorate.example: We plan to decorate the living room with new curtains and a fresh coat of paint. 我们计划用新窗帘和一层新油漆来装饰客厅。

abandon.memory_trick: 联想“a band on” → 一个乐队在台上，但主唱突然离开（abandon）了演出。
abandon.example: The captain gave the order to abandon the sinking ship. 船长下令弃船。

face.memory_trick: 想象你用手‘抚’（fa）摸自己的‘丝’（ce）滑的脸颊，记住 face 就是脸。
face.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly. 他有一种快速学习新语言的非凡能力。

gap.definition: 缺口，裂缝；间隔，差距；空白，不足
gap.memory_trick: 想象地图上两座山之间有一个‘峡谷’（gap），就是大自然留下的缺口。
gap.example: There is a significant gap between his theoretical knowledge and practical skills. (他的理论知识与实践技能之间存在显著差距。)

call.definition: v. 呼叫；打电话；命名；召集 n. 电话；呼叫；号召
call.memory_trick: 想象一只猫在夜里‘嗷’地大叫（call），吸引你注意。
call.example: She decided to call her mother every Sunday evening. 她决定每周日晚上给母亲打电话。

hamper.memory_trick: 想象一个巨大的篮子（hamper）挡住了你的去路，让你无法前进——这就是‘妨碍’的感觉。
hamper.example: The heavy rain hampered our efforts to reach the summit before dark. 大雨妨碍了我们在天黑前到达山顶的努力。

fade.definition: v. 褪色；逐渐消失；变得模糊；使褪色
fade.memory_trick: 联想 'fail'（失败）和 'dead'（死亡），失败的东西会逐渐消失，最终像死去一样褪去色彩。
fade.example: The colors of the old photograph have faded over time, but the memories remain vivid. 这张旧照片的颜色随时间褪色了，但记忆依然鲜明。

vacuum.definition: 真空；真空吸尘器；空白；空虚
vacuum.memory_trick: vac（空）+ uum（名词后缀）→ 完全空的状态 → 真空
vacuum.example: The scientist created a perfect vacuum inside the glass chamber for the experiment. （科学家在玻璃腔内制造了完美的真空用于实验。）

vacation.memory_trick: vac（空）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 空出来的时间 → 假期
vacation.example: We are planning a family vacation to the mountains next summer. （我们计划明年夏天去山区家庭度假。）

vaccine.memory_trick: vac（空）+ cine（与牛相关）→ 原指从牛痘中提取的使病毒变空的物质 → 疫苗
vaccine.example: The new vaccine has been proven effective against multiple strains of the virus. （这种新疫苗已被证明对多种病毒株有效。）

vacant.memory_trick: vac（空）+ ant（形容词后缀）→ 空的 → 空着的
vacant.example: The old house has been vacant for years, covered in dust and cobwebs. （那栋老房子空置多年，布满灰尘和蜘蛛网。）

uncover.memory_trick: un（相反）+ cover（覆盖）→ 把覆盖的东西反过来 → 揭开
uncover.example: The detective worked tirelessly to uncover the truth behind the mysterious disappearance. 侦探不知疲倦地工作，以揭开神秘失踪背后的真相。

unanimous.definition: adj. 全体一致的，一致同意的
unanimous.memory_trick: un（一个）+ anim（精神，心灵）+ ous（形容词后缀）→ 同一个心灵 → 意见一致
unanimous.example: The committee reached a unanimous decision to approve the new policy after hours of debate. 经过数小时的辩论，委员会一致决定批准这项新政策。

agony.definition: 巨大的痛苦或折磨（尤指精神或身体上的）
agony.memory_trick: 联想 'ag'（行动）+ 'ony'（像 'only'），'只有行动才能带来痛苦'，或想象一个人因持续行动而筋疲力尽、痛苦不堪。
agony.example: The patient endured the agony of the long recovery with remarkable courage. (这位病人以非凡的勇气忍受了漫长康复过程中的巨大痛苦。)

agency.definition: 代理机构；代理行为；作用力
agency.memory_trick: 词根'ag'（行动）+ 'ency'（名词后缀，表状态或机构），'行动的机构'即代理处，或理解为'代替他人行动的组织'。
agency.example: The travel agency arranged a customized tour that perfectly matched our preferences. (这家旅行社安排了一次完全符合我们喜好的定制旅行。)

agenda.definition: 议事日程；待办事项清单
agenda.memory_trick: 词根'ag'（行动）+ 'enda'（拉丁语动名词后缀，表'必须做的事'），即'需要被行动的事项清单'。
agenda.example: The first item on the agenda was a discussion about budget allocation for the upcoming project. (议程上的第一项是关于即将开展的项目预算分配的讨论。)

paradigm.memory_trick: para（旁边）+ digm（展示）→ 放在旁边展示的模型 → 范例
paradigm.example: His approach became a paradigm for future research in the field. (他的方法成为该领域未来研究的典范。)

parade.memory_trick: par（准备）+ ade（行动）→ 准备好列队行动 → 游行
parade.example: The annual parade attracted thousands of spectators along the main street. (一年一度的游行吸引了主街两旁数千名观众。)

parasite.memory_trick: para（旁边）+ site（食物）→ 在别人食物旁边蹭吃 → 寄生虫
parasite.example: The parasite feeds on the host's blood, weakening it over time. (寄生虫以宿主的血液为食，逐渐削弱宿主。)

parliament.memory_trick: parl（说话）+ iament（场所）→ 说话讨论的地方 → 议会
parliament.example: The new law was debated in parliament for several weeks before being approved. (新法律在议会辩论了数周后才获得批准。)

pardon.memory_trick: par（完全）+ don（给予）→ 完全给予（宽恕）→ 原谅
pardon.example: He asked for pardon after accidentally breaking the vase. (他无意中打碎花瓶后请求原谅。)

parcel.memory_trick: par（部分）+ cel（小）→ 分出来的一小部分 → 包裹
parcel.example: The delivery man left a parcel on the doorstep this morning. (快递员今早在门口留下了一个包裹。)

paradox.memory_trick: para（超越）+ dox（观点）→ 超越常规观点的说法 → 悖论
paradox.example: It is a paradox that the more you learn, the more you realize how little you know. (你学得越多，就越意识到自己知之甚少，这是一个悖论。)

paradise.memory_trick: para（周围）+ dise（围墙）→ 被围墙围住的完美地方 → 乐园
paradise.example: The tropical island felt like a paradise with its white sand and clear water. (这个热带岛屿拥有白色沙滩和清澈海水，宛如天堂。)

palm.definition: 手掌；棕榈树；手心；（作为胜利象征的）棕榈叶
palm.memory_trick: 想象你用手掌（palm）轻轻拍打棕榈树（palm）的树干，发出'啪'（pa）的声音，最后手掌上沾满了'木'（m）屑。
palm.example: He held the small bird gently in the palm of his hand. 他轻轻地把小鸟托在手心里。

sample.memory_trick: 谐音'三破'→ 打破三个样品才能找到完美的一个
sample.example: The researcher took a blood sample from each participant to analyze the genetic markers. 研究者从每位参与者身上抽取血样以分析遗传标记。

obedient.memory_trick: 想象一只狗听到主人说'obey'后立刻坐下，表现出 obedient（顺从）的样子。
obedient.example: The obedient student always follows the teacher's instructions without question. （这个顺从的学生总是毫无质疑地遵循老师的指示。）

obey.memory_trick: 把 'obey' 拆成 'O'（哦）和 'bay'（海湾），想象一个人在海湾边听到命令后说'哦，我服从'。
obey.example: Citizens must obey the laws to maintain social order. （公民必须遵守法律以维持社会秩序。）

obedience.memory_trick: 与 'obedient' 同源，把 'obedience' 联想为'哦，别抵赖，顺从是必须的'。
obedience.example: The dog's obedience to its owner's commands earned it a treat. （这只狗对主人命令的服从让它得到了奖励。）

wake.example: I wake up at 7 every morning.

keyboard.example: She types quickly on the keyboard.

oar.example: He pulled the oar through the water.

naughty.example: The naughty boy hid his sister's toy.

yawn.example: He couldn't stop yawning during the meeting.

lace.example: She tied her shoes with a white lace.

lad.example: The young lad helped his father in the garden.

pamphlet.example: They handed out a pamphlet about the event.

sarcastic.example: His sarcastic comment hurt her feelings.

paper.example: Please write your name on the paper.

yet.example: She hasn't finished her homework yet.

sacred.example: This temple is a sacred place.

lamb.example: The lamb followed its mother closely.

zero.example: The temperature dropped to zero last night.

pain.example: He felt a sharp pain in his back.

valve.example: The plumber replaced the broken valve.

waken.example: The noise wakened him from his sleep.

waist.example: She wore a belt around her waist.

nail.example: He hammered a nail into the wall.

sacrifice.example: Parents often make sacrifices for their children.

magnet.example: The magnet attracted the metal pins.

oath.example: He took an oath to tell the truth.

wage.example: She earns a good wage at the factory.

yell.example: He yelled for help when he fell.

pale.example: She looked pale after the illness.

sane.example: He is a sane and rational person.

parameter.example: We need to adjust the parameters of the experiment.

sack.example: He carried a sack of potatoes.

obesity.example: Obesity is a major health problem.

zeal.example: She approached her work with great zeal.

painter.example: The painter created a beautiful mural.

lamp.example: She turned on the lamp to read.

pact.example: The two countries signed a peace pact.

machine.example: The machine prints 100 pages per minute.

X-ray.example: The doctor ordered an X-ray of his chest.

paddle.example: They used a paddle to steer the canoe.

paperback.example: I prefer paperback books to hardcovers.

napkin.example: She placed a napkin on her lap.

main.example: The main road is closed for repairs.

lack.example: There is a lack of water in the desert.

naive.example: She was naive to trust him completely.

yard.example: The children played in the yard.

umbrella.example: Don't forget your umbrella; it's raining.

mainland.example: They traveled from the island to the mainland.

ugly.example: The building was old and ugly.

namely.example: He mentioned one problem, namely the cost.

naked.example: The baby was naked in the bath.

magistrate.example: The magistrate sentenced him to a fine.

nap.example: He took a short nap after lunch.

sandwich.example: She made a ham sandwich for lunch.

pack.example: He packed his suitcase for the trip.

zip.example: Please zip your jacket; it's cold.

pad.example: She wrote notes on a pad of paper.

mad.example: He was mad at his brother for breaking the toy.

parachute.example: The soldier jumped with a parachute.

package.example: The package arrived in the mail.

pant.example: He was panting after the long run.

sanction.example: The UN imposed sanctions on the country.

saddle.example: He put the saddle on the horse.

panic.example: The fire caused panic among the crowd.

pair.example: She bought a pair of shoes.

labor.example: The workers labored in the fields.

yearly.example: We have a yearly meeting in December.

maiden.example: The maiden wore a white dress.

lame.example: He gave a lame excuse for being late.

keen.example: She has a keen interest in art.

manufacture.memory_trick: manu（手）+ fact（做）+ ure → 用手做 → 制造
manufacture.example: The company plans to manufacture electric vehicles in a new eco-friendly factory. 这家公司计划在一家新的环保工厂制造电动汽车。

manuscript.memory_trick: manu（手）+ script（写）→ 手写的东西 → 手稿
manuscript.example: The author submitted his manuscript to the publisher after three years of revisions. 作者在修改三年后将手稿提交给了出版商。

manual.definition: 手册；指南；手工的；体力的
manual.memory_trick: manu（手）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 与手有关的 → 手册（用手翻阅）
manual.example: Before operating the machine, please read the safety manual carefully. 操作机器前，请仔细阅读安全手册。

manoeuvre.memory_trick: man（手）+ oeuvre（工作）→ 用手操作 → 操纵；也可联想' maneuver'（美式拼写）
manoeuvre.example: The driver had to perform a tricky manoeuvre to park in the tight space. 司机不得不做一个棘手的操作才能停进那个狭窄的空间。

obstacle.memory_trick: ob（反对）+ struct（建造）+ -acle（名词后缀）→ 在前进道路上建造反对的东西 → 障碍物。想象你正在跑步，突然有人在你面前堆了一堵墙，那就是 obstacle。
obstacle.example: The greatest obstacle to progress is often the fear of failure. (进步的最大障碍往往是害怕失败。)

obstruction.memory_trick: ob（反对）+ struct（建造）+ -ion（名词后缀）→ 建造起来反对你通行的东西 → 阻塞。想象水管里被杂物堵住，水无法流通，那就是 obstruction。
obstruction.example: The heavy snowfall caused a major obstruction on the highway, delaying commuters for hours. (大雪导致高速公路严重阻塞，通勤者被延误数小时。)

obstruct.memory_trick: ob（反对）+ struct（建造）→ 建造障碍来反对 → 阻塞。动词形式，主动地制造障碍。比如故意挡住别人的去路。
obstruct.example: Tall buildings obstruct the view of the mountains from the city center. (高楼大厦遮挡了从市中心看山的视线。)

odd.definition: 奇怪的；古怪的；单数的；零头的；偶尔的
odd.memory_trick: 联想'哦，这是奇数（odd）'，或者用'odd'和'even（偶数）'对比记忆，奇数是'多余'的那个。
odd.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which seems odd for someone who never studied abroad. （他学习新语言的能力惊人，对于一个从未出国学习的人来说，这似乎有些奇怪。）

odds.definition: 可能性；几率；优势；差额；不和
odds.memory_trick: 'odd'的复数形式，但意思转向'可能性、几率'，可以联想'奇数（odd）在赌博中常代表概率'。
odds.example: The odds of winning the lottery are extremely low, but that doesn't stop millions from trying. （中彩票的几率极低，但这并不能阻止数百万人尝试。）

impatient.definition: 不耐烦的；急躁的；无法忍受的
impatient.memory_trick: im-（否定）+ patient（耐心的）= 没有耐心的，即'不耐烦的'。想象一个人不停看表、跺脚的样子。
impatient.example: He became impatient waiting for the bus, pacing back and forth on the sidewalk. 他等公交车时变得不耐烦，在人行道上踱来踱去。

hard.definition: 硬的；困难的；努力的；严厉的
hard.memory_trick: 想象一块坚硬的石头（hard rock），你用力敲打它，它却纹丝不动，记住'硬'就是hard。
hard.example: The diamond is one of the hardest natural materials known to man. (钻石是人类已知的最硬天然材料之一。)

hardware.memory_trick: hard（硬的）+ ware（器具）= 硬件，想象电脑中那些坚硬的金属零件就是hardware。
hardware.example: The company specializes in manufacturing computer hardware components. (这家公司专门制造计算机硬件组件。)

hardly.memory_trick: hard（困难地）+ ly（副词后缀）= 几乎不，想象做一件事困难到几乎无法完成，就是hardly。
hardly.example: She could hardly believe her eyes when she saw the surprise party. (当她看到惊喜派对时，几乎不敢相信自己的眼睛。)

hardship.memory_trick: hard（困难的）+ ship（状态）= 困难的状态，即困苦。想象一个人航行在暴风雨中，经历重重困难就是hardship。
hardship.example: The family endured great hardship during the economic crisis. (这个家庭在经济危机期间经历了巨大的困苦。)

harden.memory_trick: hard（硬的）+ en（使动后缀）= 使变硬。想象水泥从软变硬的过程就是harden。
harden.example: The steel must be heated and then cooled quickly to harden it properly. (钢材必须加热然后快速冷却才能适当硬化。)

pedal.memory_trick: ped（脚）+ al（后缀）= 用脚操作的东西 → 踏板
pedal.example: She pressed the pedal gently to accelerate the car smoothly. 她轻轻踩下油门踏板，让汽车平稳加速。

pedestrian.definition: 行人；步行的；缺乏想象力的
pedestrian.memory_trick: ped（脚）+ estrian（复合后缀）= 用脚走路的人 → 行人
pedestrian.example: The pedestrian waited patiently at the crosswalk for the light to turn green. 行人在人行横道耐心等待绿灯亮起。

scrutiny.definition: 仔细而彻底的检查或审视；细察
scrutiny.memory_trick: 想象一个侦探（s）在暗处（cru）用探照灯（tiny）仔细检查每个角落，不放过任何细节。
scrutiny.example: The scientist placed the data under intense scrutiny to ensure no errors remained. 科学家对数据进行了严密的审查，以确保没有遗留任何错误。

scissors.definition: 剪刀，一种用于切割纸张、布料等的两刃工具
scissors.memory_trick: 想象用剪刀（scissors）剪开（sciss）一张纸，声音像'撕纸'（siz）
scissors.example: She carefully trimmed the edges of the photograph with a pair of scissors. 她小心地用剪刀修剪了照片的边缘。

heroine.memory_trick: hero（英雄）+ ine（阴性后缀）= 女性英雄，就像电影中的神奇女侠。
heroine.example: The novel's heroine overcomes countless obstacles to find her true love. 这部小说的女主角克服了无数障碍，找到了真爱。

heroic.memory_trick: hero（英雄）+ ic（形容词后缀）= 英雄般的，像超级英雄一样勇敢。
heroic.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a heroic effort for someone his age. 他拥有快速学习新语言的非凡能力，对于他这个年龄的人来说，这是一种英雄般的努力。

verge.definition: n. 边缘，边界；v. 濒临，接近（某种状态）
verge.memory_trick: 想象一个斜坡（verg）的顶端，你站在'边缘'（verge）上，再往前一步就会'倾斜'下去。
verge.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his progress often verges on the extraordinary. (他有着快速学习新语言的非凡能力，他的进步常常近乎超常。)

telegraph.definition: 电报；电报机；用电报发送（信息）
telegraph.memory_trick: tele（远距离）+ graph（书写）→ 远距离书写 → 电报
telegraph.example: The invention of the telegraph revolutionized long-distance communication in the 19th century. 电报的发明在19世纪彻底改变了远距离通信。

imperative.definition: 必要的；紧急的；命令式的；祈使语气
imperative.memory_trick: im（进入）+ per（完全）+ ative（形容词后缀）→ 进入完全命令状态→ 必须做的，紧急的。
imperative.example: It is imperative that we finish the report before the deadline. 我们在截止日期前完成报告是至关重要的。

imperial.definition: 帝国的；皇帝的；威严的；宏大的
imperial.memory_trick: imper（命令、统治）+ ial（形容词后缀）→ 与统治相关的→ 帝国的，皇帝的。
imperial.example: The imperial palace stood as a symbol of centuries-old authority. 这座皇宫矗立着，象征着数百年的权威。

odor.memory_trick: 联想'欧德'（谐音）——欧洲的老德（德国）啤酒有种特殊的气味，记住这个词就是记住那种独特的味道。
odor.example: The kitchen was filled with the pleasant odor of freshly baked bread. 厨房里弥漫着新鲜出炉面包的宜人气味。

sector.memory_trick: sect（切割）+ or（名词后缀）→ 被切割出来的部分 → 部门或扇形区域。想象一个圆形蛋糕被切成一块块扇形，每一块就是一个 sector。
sector.example: The technology sector has seen rapid growth over the past decade. (科技部门在过去十年中经历了快速增长。)

secondary.memory_trick: sec（切割）+ ond（序数词根）+ ary（形容词后缀）→ 从主要部分切割出来的 → 次要的。想象主路和支路，支路就是 secondary road。
secondary.example: Her primary concern is safety, while cost is only a secondary consideration. (她首要关心的是安全，而成本只是次要考虑因素。)

section.memory_trick: sect（切割）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 切割后的结果 → 部分或章节。想象一本书被切割成不同章节，每一章就是一个 section。
section.example: Please read the first section of the report before the meeting. (请在会议前阅读报告的第一部分。)

telescope.definition: 望远镜；一种用于观察远处物体的光学仪器
telescope.memory_trick: tele（远）+ scope（观察）→ 远距离观察的工具，想象伽利略仰望星空的样子。
telescope.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly. 中文翻译：他具有快速学习新语言的非凡能力。

tell.definition: 告诉；讲述；辨别；命令；产生效果
tell.memory_trick: 联想'tell'与'tale'同源，想象你在'讲述'（tell）一个古老的'故事'（tale），两者发音相似，帮助记忆。
tell.example: Can you tell me the truth about what happened last night? 你能告诉我昨晚发生了什么真相吗？

tease.definition: 取笑；戏弄；挑逗；梳理（头发）
tease.memory_trick: 'tease'与'tell'同源，但词义演变：从'讲述'到'用言语挑逗或戏弄'，想象一个人'讲述'（tell）一些玩笑话来'戏弄'（tease）别人。
tease.example: The children like to tease their little brother by hiding his toys. 孩子们喜欢通过藏起弟弟的玩具来逗弄他。

seal.definition: n. 印章；封条；海豹；vt. 密封；盖章；决定
seal.memory_trick: 想象在信封上盖一个圆形印章（seal），印章的图案是一只海豹（seal）的剪影，记住 'seal' 既是印章也是海豹。
seal.example: The ambassador placed his official seal on the treaty document to confirm the agreement. 大使在条约文件上盖了他的官方印章以确认协议。

velocity.definition: 速度，速率（尤指沿特定方向的矢量速率）
velocity.memory_trick: 联想 'veloc'（快速）+ 'ity'（名词后缀）→ 速度就是'快'的程度；也可记作've（车辆） + loc（位置）'→ 车辆位置变化快慢。
velocity.example: The car reached a velocity of 120 kilometers per hour on the highway. 这辆汽车在高速公路上达到了每小时120公里的速度。

harassment.memory_trick: 拆解为 'harass'（骚扰）+ 'ment'（名词后缀），想象一个人被不断'哈拉'（harass）追着跑，最终变成'门特'（ment）般的心理阴影。

example.en: The company has a strict policy against any form of workplace harassment.
example.zh: 公司对任何形式的工作场所骚扰行为都有严格的政策。

harbour.definition: 港口；避难所；庇护；心怀（情感）
harbour.memory_trick: 想象一艘船在暴风雨中驶入一个宁静的港口（harbour），就像在酒吧（bar）里找到庇护所，harbour = har（像her） + bour（像bar），记住'她的酒吧'就是安全的避风港。
harbour.example: The old fisherman decided to harbour the stray cat in his small boat shed. 老渔夫决定在他的小船棚里庇护那只流浪猫。

scrape.definition: v. 刮，擦；刮去；擦伤；n. 刮擦声；擦伤；困境
scrape.memory_trick: 想象一只猫在木地板上'scrape'（刮）它的爪子，发出刺耳的声音，同时地面上留下'scrap'（碎片）。
scrape.example: He had to scrape the ice off the car windshield before driving to work this morning. 他今天早上开车上班前，不得不把冰从汽车挡风玻璃上刮掉。

scrap.definition: n. 碎片，小块；废料，废品；争吵；v. 废弃，抛弃；打架
scrap.memory_trick: 把'scrap'拆成's'+'crap'，'crap'是废物，所以'scrap'就是'一点点废物'——碎片、废料。
scrap.example: The old car was sold to a junkyard for scrap metal after it broke down completely. 那辆旧车彻底抛锚后，被卖给了废品站当作废金属处理。

tear.memory_trick: 想象一张纸被两只手用力向两边拉扯，发出'tear'的声音，就是撕裂的动作。
tear.example: She carefully tore the letter into small pieces to ensure no one could read it. 她小心地将信撕成碎片，以确保没人能读到它。

lane.memory_trick: 想象一条狭窄的巷子（lane）里，只有一盏灯（lamp）照亮，记住 'lane' 和 'lamp' 发音相似但含义不同。
lane.example: He turned into a narrow lane behind the old church, where the cobblestones were slick with rain. (他拐进老教堂后面一条狭窄的小巷，那里的鹅卵石被雨水打得很滑。)

occasion.definition: 时刻，场合；重大活动；机会，时机
occasion.memory_trick: 拆解为 'occ'（发生）+ 'asion'（像行动），想象某个行动发生的特定时刻。
occasion.example: He seized the occasion to express his gratitude to the team. 他抓住这个机会向团队表达了感谢。

occasional.definition: 偶尔的，间或发生的；临时的
occasional.memory_trick: 由 'occasion'（场合）加 '-al'（形容词后缀）构成，表示只在某些场合才发生的，即偶尔的。
occasional.example: He makes occasional trips to the countryside to relax. 他偶尔去乡下放松一下。

lap.definition: （跑道的）一圈；（旅程的）一段；膝部（坐下时大腿的上方）；重叠部分
lap.memory_trick: 想象小猫蜷缩在你的膝盖（lap）上，形成一个温暖的'圈'，同时它的尾巴轻轻'重叠'在你的手臂上。
lap.example: He completed the final lap of the race with remarkable speed. 他以惊人的速度完成了比赛的最后一圈。

veil.memory_trick: 想象一位新娘戴着白色面纱（veil）走过教堂，面纱遮住了她的脸，就像单词中的字母 'v' 形似两片垂下的布料，中间藏着新娘的容颜。
veil.example: The bride lifted her delicate lace veil to kiss her groom, revealing a radiant smile. 新娘掀起精致的蕾丝面纱亲吻新郎，露出了灿烂的笑容。

vein.definition: 静脉；叶脉；矿脉；纹理；风格
vein.memory_trick: 想象一条蓝色的血管（vein）在皮肤下蜿蜒，像河流（vein 与 rain 押韵）一样流淌。
vein.example: The geologist discovered a rich vein of gold deep underground. 地质学家在地下深处发现了一条丰富的金矿脉。

seam.definition: n. 缝，接缝；v. 缝合，接合
seam.memory_trick: 想象'sea'（大海）和'm'（像波浪），大海的波浪像一条缝线连接着海面。
seam.example: The tailor carefully pressed the seam of the dress to make it lie flat. 裁缝仔细熨平裙子的接缝，使其平整。

lash.definition: 鞭打；猛击；捆绑；睫毛；严厉谴责
lash.memory_trick: 想象一根鞭子（lash）像闪电（flash）一样快速抽打，注意'l'和'ash'组合仿佛鞭子划破空气的咻咻声。
lash.example: The storm lashed the coast with fierce winds and heavy rain. 暴风雨以狂风暴雨猛烈袭击了海岸。

scold.example: She scolded her son for breaking the window.

impetus.example: The new policy gave an impetus to economic growth.

versatile.example: She is a versatile musician who can play several instruments.

vertical.example: The vertical line divides the page into two columns.
